designs.summary: The tcxo driver has NO numeric Config struct: new() takes only the Tcxo<'d> token and the count registers are read-only, so there is no caller-supplies-an-overflowing-value newtype surface like PWM's Duty/PwmPeriod. The defects are (1+2) class-C unenforced init preconditions — new() neither self-enables the free-running counter (tcxo_status.enable, bit 2) nor clears it (bit 1), so 'construct -> running, zero-based counter' does NOT hold the way PWM's configure self-enables its clock tree; (3) a class-A read-path width hazard — read_counter/read_counter32 use raw .bits() (full u32) on each count register instead of the PAC's 16-bit-masked .countN() accessor, so if silicon leaves bits[31:16] non-zero the upper half of one lane bleeds into the next; (4+5) two class-C handshake gaps — a 100-iteration poll budget justified by a wrong 32kHz figure (TCXO actually runs 24/40MHz) and a refresh/valid handshake that never re-arms the valid flag, risking a sticky stale read. The dominant, type-expressible fix is to make new() self-enable+clear the counter (the actual run-gate, since TCXO has no separate CKEN register) so the counter is guaranteed running by construction; the read-path width fix is a one-line switch to .countN(); the timeout/race fixes are doc-and-guard plus a saner budget. OPEN QUESTIONS needing on-board measurement: (a) do count bits[31:16] read as zero on silicon (decides whether the .countN() mask is cosmetic or load-bearing); (b) does tcxo_status.valid auto-clear on a new refresh request or is it sticky (decides whether a re-arm is mandatory); (c) the true latch latency at 24/40MHz to size the poll budget. The connected WS63 HIL suite can drive register-level checks for all of these (read raw tcxo_countN bits[31:16] post-refresh; observe valid before/after a refresh; time the latch) so the chosen bounds can be validated and the doc-and-guard items upgraded to hard guarantees once measured.

designs.surface: new() — counter-enable precondition (tcxo_status.enable, bit 2 / TCXO_EN_BIT)
designs.validation_bounds: Self-enable is unconditional: new() writes (status | TCXO_EN_BIT) where TCXO_EN_BIT = 1<<2 (tcxo_status.enable bit 2, regs_def.h:53), matching vendor hal_tcxo_v150.c:19-30 set_enable. read_counter() is only reachable in state Enabled (enable bit known-set), so the accept predicate is structural: a counter read requires enable==1 by construction; there is no runtime value to range-check. RMW preserves all other status bits (verified by in-file proptest enable_sets_only_enable_bit).
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait is involved — tcxo exposes no embedded-hal trait (the count registers are read-only outputs; there is no Read/SetDutyCycle/etc. impl on this driver). All trait impls (none) stay untouched. The default generic param S=Enabled keeps the common path ergonomic.

designs.surface: new() — counter-clear precondition (tcxo_status.clear, bit 1 / TCXO_CLEAR_BIT)
designs.validation_bounds: new() writes (status | TCXO_CLEAR_BIT) where TCXO_CLEAR_BIT = 1<<1 (tcxo_status.clear bit 1, regs_def.h:52) immediately after the enable write, mirroring vendor init order (enable then clear, hal_tcxo_v150.c:19-30). Accept predicate is structural: post-construction the counter origin is defined (==0 base). RMW preserves other bits (proptest clear_and_refresh_set_only_their_bit). On-board check: read_counter() right after new() returns a small, monotonically increasing value, not a large stale latch.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait involved; no trait impls change. Pure inherent-method init step inside new().

designs.surface: read_counter / read_counter32 — count0..count3 read width (.bits() u32 vs 16-bit count field)
designs.validation_bounds: Each lane masked to [15:0]: value & 0xFFFF, i.e. accept 0..=0xFFFF per lane (regs_def.h:35 tcxo_count_data.data:16; vendor masks via tmp_count.b.data, hal_tcxo_v150_regs_op.c:44-54; PAC Count0R..Count3R = FieldReader<u16> with (bits & 0xffff)). Reject/strip any bit in [31:16] of each count register. This is the exact 16-bit truncation the driver currently omits (.bits() at tcxo.rs:97-101 / 109-111). NEEDS ON-BOARD CONFIRMATION whether silicon ever sets bits[31:16]; if it does, this fix is load-bearing, otherwise cosmetic — but masking is harmless either way and matches the vendor.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait involved; signatures of read_counter/read_counter32 are unchanged (still -> Option<u64>/Option<u32>). Pure internal correctness fix.

designs.surface: refresh — valid-flag poll timeout (100 iterations; comment claims 32kHz, real TCXO is 24/40MHz)
designs.validation_bounds: Accept budget n in 1..=0xFFFFFFFF (RefreshTimeout::iters rejects 0). DEFAULT = 10_000 iterations, derived from TCXO = 24MHz (tcxo_porting.h:21); VENDOR_MAX = 0xFFFFFFFF (TCXO_LOCK_GET_ATTE, tcxo_porting.h:29). The current literal 100 with a 32kHz rationale (tcxo.rs:75-76) is the defect; vendor polls up to 0xFFFFFFFF (hal_tcxo_v150.c:44-49). NEEDS ON-BOARD CONFIRMATION of true latch latency at 24/40MHz to pin the DEFAULT; until then DEFAULT is deliberately generous so a healthy refresh cannot spuriously time out.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait involved; refresh is private and read_counter keeps its Option return. The newtype is an inherent-method config value, free to type (the operational read signature stays fixed).

designs.surface: refresh — request bit not self-cleared / valid flag not re-armed before poll (stale-read race)
designs.validation_bounds: No numeric bound — this is a precondition/race, not a value range. Current code (tcxo.rs:70-83) sets TCXO_REFRESH_BIT (1<<0) and polls TCXO_VALID_BIT (1<<4, regs_def.h:55) with no re-arm; vendor set_refresh is RMW leaving the bit set (hal_tcxo_v150_regs_op.h:67-73) and re-reads valid each loop (hal_tcxo_v150.c:44-49). The accept/reject rule once measured: if valid auto-clears on a new refresh request, the current poll is correct (accept first-valid==fresh); if valid is sticky, require an observed low->high edge before accepting the count (reject a read where valid was already 1 at entry). Decided by HIL note (b).
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait involved; refresh is private and read_counter's Option signature is unchanged. Doc-and-guard adds no public type, so it composes with the type-state and timeout-newtype changes above.

designs.summary: The SPI Config struct (spi.rs:18-29) has three fields; SpiMode is already a closed 4-variant enum that maps only to valid CTRA scph/scpol combos and needs no change. The two high-severity defects are both on `data_bits: u8`: (A) any value >=33 overflows the 5-bit dfs32 field (bits 13:17) and spills bit18 into the adjacent CTRA.trsm field, silently corrupting transfer mode (e.g. 33 -> DFS=0 + trsm=1/TxOnly so reads return zeros); (B) even within the field only 8 and 32 are vendor-supported, and the driver's data path is byte-only (`write[i] as u32` push / `rx as u8` read at spi.rs:169/173/175/185/207/211), so anything but 8 silently mis-transmits on the wire. I replace `data_bits: u8` with a `DataBits` enum that, for the shipped byte-only data path, exposes only `DataBits::Eight` (an explicit `from_u8`/`new` returning `Option` accepts only 8 and rejects everything else) — this makes both A and B unrepresentable in one move. `frequency: u32` becomes a `Frequency` newtype whose fallible constructor returns None instead of silently clamping (defect D). The two clock-tree preconditions are class C: WS63 already self-enables its source clock + gate in configure_spi_source_clock (keep, harden the PLL-locked precondition as doc-and-guard since "PLL up" is a board/clock_init fact a type cannot express); BS2X has NO source-clock/gate setup ported and SPI_CLOCK_HZ is an unconfirmed TODO value, so BS2X SPI config must be gated off (compile-error / cfg-disabled) until silicon bring-up rather than silently programming a wrong/un-gated clock. OPEN QUESTION needing on-board measurement: whether 32-bit frames are usable at all given the byte-only FIFO loops — they are NOT today (each FIFO slot gets 1 byte), so DataBits deliberately does NOT expose 32 until both a frame-width-aware data path AND a HIL check exist; do not widen to 16/24 (vendor marks them "Not supported now"). Also unconfirmed: the BS2X SPI_CLOCK_HZ=32MHz value and whether BS2X needs its own gate sequence — both require the fbb_bs2x port + board.

designs.surface: Config.data_bits: u8 (new_spi0 / new_spi1 / configure_spi) -> SPI_CTRA.dfs32 field overflow into trsm (defect A)
designs.validation_bounds: ACCEPT iff bits == 8 (DataBits::Eight). REJECT all of 0,1..=7, 9..=31, 32, 33..=255. Register bound: dfs32 is FieldWriter<_,5> at CTRA bits 13:17 (PAC lib.rs:18931, :19021-19024) and trsm is bits 18:19 (:19026); the accepted value yields dfs32()=7 (0..=31), so the `<<13` write stays inside bits 13:17 and never reaches bit18. Vendor floor: hal_spi_v151.c:85 rejects every frame_size != _8(0x07) && != _32(0x1F); byte-only data path (spi.rs:169/173/185/207/211) further restricts the runnable set to 8 only.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Pure config-surface change on inherent constructors (new_spi0/new_spi1/configure_spi). The embedded-hal `spi::SpiBus<u8>` and `embedded_hal_async::spi::SpiBus<u8>` impls (spi.rs:230-268, 343-366) and `spi::ErrorType`/`Error` are untouched — they remain &[u8]/&mut [u8] + Result, which is exactly the 8-bit-frame contract DataBits::Eight enforces, so the operational surface stays standard.

designs.surface: Config.data_bits: u8 valid-silicon-values (only 8 and 32 vendor-supported; driver byte-only so only 8 runs) (defect B)
designs.validation_bounds: ACCEPT only 8 today. Vendor whitelist is {8, 32} (hal_spi.h:196-205: _8=0x07, _32=0x1F; _16=0x0F/_24=0x17 both "Not supported now"; hal_spi_v151.c:85). Data-path floor narrows the *runnable* set to {8}: each FIFO slot is loaded with one byte (`byte as u32`) and read as one byte (`rx as u8`), so a >8-bit frame right-justifies/zero-pads and only the low byte goes on the wire. Therefore the shipped accept-predicate is `bits == 8`; widening to 32 is deferred behind a data-path change + HIL confirmation.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Unchanged from the A design: SpiBus<u8> blocking + async impls stay byte-oriented and untouched; DataBits::Eight is precisely the width those &[u8] signatures already assume. No trait signature changes.

designs.surface: Config.frequency: u32 -> SPI_BRS.clk_div / SCKDV silent clamp [2,0xFFFE] (defect D)
designs.validation_bounds: ACCEPT iff 2 <= SPI_CLOCK_HZ/hz <= 0xFFFE. Bounds from PAC clk_div = FieldWriter<_,16,u16> bits 0:15 (lib.rs:19209-19211) and vendor caps HAL_SPI_MINUMUM_CLK_DIV=2 / HAL_SPI_MAXIMUM_CLK_DIV=65534 (hal_spi_v151_regs_def.h:172-173, spi_porting.c:395-402). On WS63 (SRC=160MHz) the runnable window is ~2.44 kHz (div 0xFFFE) .. 80 MHz (div 2). REJECT freq=0, freq >= SRC/2 (would clamp to div 2), and freq so low that div > 0xFFFE — the cases the old code silently collapsed.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Config-only newtype on the constructors; no embedded-hal surface. SpiBus impls do not see frequency. Severity is medium/class D, so this is the lowest-priority of the three Config changes but follows the same 'return None instead of clamp' rule as PWM.

designs.surface: configure_spi BS2X build: SPI source clock + CKEN gate never set up; SPI_CLOCK_HZ is an unconfirmed TODO value (defect C)
designs.validation_bounds: Not a register-range predicate — a build-time precondition. Evidence: spi.rs:142-143 source-clock call is chip-ws63 only; spi.rs:135-141 explicitly defers BS2X source clock; soc/bs21.rs:37-38 SPI_CLOCK_HZ=32MHz 'assumed'; vendor WS63 gate sequence CKEN_CTL1 bit25 (spi_porting.c:695-697) has no ported BS2X analogue. Guard rejects every BS2X SPI build that has not explicitly opted into the unproven path.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No effect on embedded-hal impls (they are chip-neutral). This only gates construction on BS2X; WS63 (where the gate IS self-enabled, the class-C 'self-enable own clock gate' requirement already met by configure_spi_source_clock) is unaffected.

designs.surface: configure_spi_source_clock (WS63): CLDO_CRG div [1,0x1F] + TCXO->PLL switch assumes 480MHz FNPLL already locked (defect C)
designs.validation_bounds: Two guards: (1) const-assert that SPI_PLL_ROOT_MHZ(480) / SPI_CLOCK_HZ_MHz <= 0x1F so the 5-bit CRG divider (spi_porting.c:204 SPI_DIV_LEN=5, mask 0x1F, spi.rs:113-114) never silently saturates/aliases — holds for the shipped 160MHz (div=3); fails the build if someone sets SPI_CLOCK_HZ < ~15.5MHz. (2) runtime debug_assert PLL-locked before the TCXO->PLL switch (CLK_SEL bit6, spi.rs:130). The bounded wait_until already turns a no-clock condition into SpiError::Timeout at the operational layer, so a missed precondition is signalled, never an infinite hang.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal change; SpiError::Timeout (already an embedded_hal::spi::Error via ErrorKind::Other at spi.rs:222-226) is the existing channel through which a not-locked-PLL failure surfaces from SpiBus transfers. The doc-and-guard only hardens the inherent clock-setup fn.

designs.summary: The RTC's deploy-blocking defect is class-C but UNLIKE PWM/I2S it is NOT a clock-gate the driver can flip itself: the v100 RTC at 0x5702_4000 is an AON block with no CKEN bit anywhere (absent from clock.rs Peripheral enum, rtc_porting.c has no enable sequence), driven directly by the external 32.768 kHz crystal. So "self-enable its own clock gate" does NOT apply here — there is nothing to gate; the only honest fix for the missing clock is to move construction behind a precondition the BSP must vouch for. The tightening therefore is: (1) a doc-and-guard fallible constructor `RtcDriver::new(rtc) -> Option<Self>` returning Some only after a liveness probe (two current_value reads with a bounded settle), plus an `unsafe new_assume_clocked` escape hatch for BSPs that already know the crystal is populated — types cannot express "a 32 kHz crystal is soldered on", so a guard + Option is the correct tool; (2) collapse RtcMode to drop the unattested Periodic/mode=1 variant down to a single FreeRunning (enum-with-one-value / or remove the mode arg) since the vendor never programs mode=1; (3) a validated `RtcReload(u32)` newtype that is non-zero (and a `try_from_duration`/`try_from_ms` Option ctor) so load_value=0 is unrepresentable, mirroring PwmPeriod; (4) make `set_load` stop->write->restart internally (bracket the reload like every vendor path) so the live-reload glitch precondition is enforced, not documented. The doc-fiction RTC_COUNTER_WIDTH=48 / module doc "48-bit" stays a comment fix only — the registers and the u32 API width are correct. OPEN QUESTION needing on-board measurement: every behavioral claim (does mode=1 do anything; does a live set_load glitch; does the bus actually stall on the unpopulated EVB; what counter width silicon really exposes) is inferred from the vendor SDK + bring-up notes — none can be confirmed until an EVB with the 32.768 kHz crystal populated is available, because on THIS board any RTC register access hangs the core, so even the liveness probe in (1) can itself hang and may need a watchdog/timeout backstop at the BSP level.

designs.surface: RtcDriver::new(rtc) — construction / clock-source (32.768 kHz crystal) precondition (defect class C)
designs.validation_bounds: ACCEPT iff the free-running counter advances during the probe: `current_value()` second read != first read after RTC_PROBE_SETTLE_ITERS spin iterations. REJECT (None) iff the two reads are equal (counter stuck => no 32.768 kHz clock => crystal unpopulated). new_assume_clocked bypasses the predicate entirely (BSP-asserted). Bound source: CONFIG_RTC_CLOCK_VALUE=32768 means a live counter MUST tick at 32_768 Hz, so across any non-zero settle window two reads of a clocked counter differ; an unclocked counter is frozen. The exact RTC_PROBE_SETTLE_ITERS value needs on-board calibration (one 32 kHz tick ≈ 30.5 µs).
designs.embedded_hal_note: RTC has no embedded-hal trait impls (it is an inherent-method driver: configure/enable/current_value/interrupt_*), so changing new() to return Option touches no trait. No embedded-hal surface is affected.

designs.surface: RtcDriver::configure(mode, ..) — mode = RtcMode::Periodic (control bit 1 = RTC_MODE_USER_DEF, unattested) (defect class B)
designs.validation_bounds: ACCEPT only mode = FreeRunning => control bit1 = 0 (RTC_MODE_FREE_RUN). REJECT (make unrepresentable) mode = Periodic => control bit1 = 1 (RTC_MODE_USER_DEF), which no vendor path programs. Bound source: hal_rtc_v100.h enum { RTC_MODE_FREE_RUN=0, RTC_MODE_USER_DEF=1 } + hal_rtc_v100.c hal_rtc_v100_config_load always calls set_mode(RTC_MODE_FREE_RUN). PAC rtc_control.mode is a single bit. Whether mode=1 does anything usable needs on-board confirmation before any UserDefined variant is reintroduced.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait involved; RtcMode/configure are inherent. Removing the Periodic variant (or the mode arg) changes no trait impl.

designs.surface: RtcDriver::configure / set_load — load_value = 0 silently accepted (defect class D)
designs.validation_bounds: ACCEPT count in 1..=u32::MAX (RTC_LOAD_COUNT is a real 32-bit reg). REJECT count == 0. For try_from_ms: ACCEPT iff 1 <= ms*32768/1000 <= u32::MAX, else None (sub-tick rounds to 0, or > 2^32 ticks ≈ 36.4 h). Bound source: vendor uapi_rtc_start (rtc.c:461) and uapi_rtc_start_hw_rtc (rtc.c:575) both reject rtc_ms==0 with ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAM; PAC rtc_load_count is u32 single reg.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait; configure/set_load are inherent. Swapping the u32 param for RtcReload changes no trait impl.

designs.surface: RtcDriver::set_load — reload written to a live (enabled) counter without stop/reload bracketing (defect class C)
designs.validation_bounds: INVARIANT enforced (not a value bound): RTC_LOAD_COUNT is only written while CTRL_ENABLE (bit0) is 0; if the counter was running, set_load disables it (clear bit0), writes load_count, then restores the prior control word. ACCEPT all RtcReload inputs (already non-zero by the newtype); the guard is on ORDERING, not on the value. Bound source: vendor rtc.c:486-488 / 580-586 / 651-653 always do hal_rtc_stop; rtc_load_cycle_with_mask; hal_rtc_start. Whether the live-reload glitch is observable, and whether stop/start is mandatory on silicon, needs scope confirmation on a clocked EVB.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait; set_load is inherent. The internal stop/write/restart sequence changes no trait impl and the signature change is only the RtcReload param (shared with the class-D fix).

designs.surface: Module doc + RTC_COUNTER_WIDTH=48 (soc/ws63.rs:150, rtc.rs module doc) — '48-bit counter' doc fiction
designs.validation_bounds: No runtime predicate (doc/const only). The register-level fact: RTC_LOAD_COUNT and RTC_CURRENT_VALUE are 32-bit (Ux=u32) — accept/reject does not apply; the u32 API width already matches the registers. Open: the real silicon counter width (32 vs documented 48) needs on-board measurement.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait; this is a doc/const change only, touches no API and no trait impl.

designs.summary: The headline tightening is a construct-time-validated `WdtTimeout` newtype that replaces the raw `timeout_ms: u32` argument to `configure`, exactly mirroring how `PwmPeriod`/`Duty` make `PwmChannel::configure` infallible. Its `from_ms(u32) -> Option<Self>` rejects (a) `timeout_ms == 0` — which today programs WDT_LOAD=0 / instant-reset (class B) — and (b) any value whose load field would exceed the 24-bit WDT_LOAD max, replacing the dangerous silent clamp to ~179 s (WS63) / ~134 s (BS2X) that fires the watchdog SOONER than asked (class D, the high-severity defect). Because the constructor pre-validates, `configure` becomes a total function that always programs the requested window or never compiles, and the misleading `zero_timeout_yields_zero_load` / `large_timeout_clamps_to_max_field` tests get replaced by constructor-rejection tests. The per-chip max bound is derived from the clock: max_ms = (0xFFFFFF<<8)*1000/WDT_CLOCK_HZ = 178956 @24 MHz, 134217 @32 MHz. The class C clock-gate gap (AON_CRG_CKEN_CTL.wdt_gate[4] / wdt_soft_rst[1]) and the class B BS2X clock-rate assumption are doc-and-guard, not types: the AON WDT gate is reset-default-on and the vendor turnon hook is a no-op, so unlike PWM there is nothing to self-enable on the normal path — but I add an optional `#[cfg(feature=\"chip-ws63\")]` `enable_wdt_clock()` guard plus a documented precondition. OPEN QUESTION needing on-board measurement: the BS2X WDT counting clock is assumed = 32 MHz TCXO but is UNVERIFIED (bs21.rs literally carries `TODO(bs21): confirm`, and no fbb_bs2x watchdog porting exists in-tree); if BS2X actually counts at a divided/24 MHz rate, every timeout_ms AND the 134217 ms max bound are mis-scaled. The WS63 HIL suite can validate the WS63 bound and rejection logic at register-poll level today; the BS2X bound needs a BS2X board.

designs.surface: configure(timeout_ms: u32, ...) — the ms->WDT_LOAD 24-bit field conversion that silently clamps to WDT_MAX_LOAD (line 119 .min(WDT_MAX_LOAD)), firing the watchdog sooner than requested (class D, high)
designs.validation_bounds: ACCEPT iff ms != 0 AND 1 <= (ms*WDT_CLOCK_HZ/1000)>>8 <= 0xFFFFFF. Equivalently ms in 1..=MAX_MS where MAX_MS = (0xFFFFFF<<8)*1000/WDT_CLOCK_HZ = 178_956 @24MHz (WS63), 134_217 @32MHz (BS2X), 107_374 @40MHz boards. REJECT ms==0 (vendor set_attr `timeout==0` -> ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAM, and TOP_MIN_CLKS=0xFF floor) and ms>MAX_MS (vendor `timeout_s > TOP_MAX_CLKS` / overflow -> ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAM). Note: the `load==0` check subsumes the TOP_MIN floor since ms>=1 already yields ~24000/32000 cycles >> 0xFF, so sub-TOP_MIN is unreachable via the ms API except at ms==0.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait is involved. WDT exposes no embedded-hal trait impl (there is no `embedded-hal` watchdog trait in 1.0; feed/enable/disable/configure are all inherent methods). configure is an inherent config method, so it is free to take a typed argument. embedded-hal-nb / digital / io impls: none on this driver, all untouched.

designs.surface: configure timeout_ms == 0 -> WDT_LOAD = 0 (instant/immediate-reset watchdog; codified by the `zero_timeout_yields_zero_load` test) (class B, medium)
designs.validation_bounds: REJECT ms == 0 (and any load that would compute to 0). The smallest accepted input is ms == 1, which at every supported clock (>=24 MHz) yields >= 24000 cycles, comfortably above the vendor TOP_MIN_CLKS = 0xFF = 255-cycle floor and above the 256-cycle (field>=1) threshold. So a single zero-check at the ms boundary fully enforces the vendor minimum -- no separate min-ms constant is needed.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Same as the max-bound design -- one shared WdtTimeout newtype, no embedded-hal trait touched.

designs.surface: new()/configure() never enable AON_CRG_CKEN_CTL.wdt_gate[4] or release AON_SOFT_RST_CTL.wdt_soft_rst[1] -- 'construct -> clocked' is an unenforced precondition (class C, low)
designs.validation_bounds: Not a value-range predicate. Guard action: set AON_SOFT_RST_CTL[1]=0 (release) then AON_CRG_CKEN_CTL[4]=1 (gate on). This differs from PWM's mandatory self-enable: the WDT gate is reset-default-on and the vendor hook is a no-op, so the guard is defensive/idempotent rather than required for first-boot function. The exact AON_CRG register offsets must be pulled from ws63-pac (lib.rs:25257-25274 wdt_gate, :25339-25356 wdt_soft_rst) before committing -- left as ???? in the sketch. BS2X body intentionally omitted (AON CRG layout unconfirmed in-tree).
designs.embedded_hal_note: Internal clock helper, no public signature change beyond configure already taking WdtTimeout. No embedded-hal trait involved.

designs.surface: WDT_CLOCK_HZ = soc::chip::TCXO_HZ assumed = 32 MHz on BS2X/BS21 (unverified vs vendor) -- mis-scales every timeout_ms and the MAX_MS bound if wrong (class B, medium)
designs.validation_bounds: No runtime predicate -- this is a correctness-of-constant concern, not an input-validation one. The constant directly parameterizes WdtTimeout::MAX_MS and the cycles math: WS63 24 MHz -> MAX_MS 178_956 (vendor-confirmed); BS2X 32 MHz -> MAX_MS 134_217 (UNCONFIRMED, depends on the unverified clock). Accept/reject bound for the newtype is correct *given* WDT_CLOCK_HZ; the open question is whether WDT_CLOCK_HZ itself is right for BS2X.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Doc + optional cfg tripwire only. No signature or trait change; no embedded-hal impl involved.

designs.summary: The uart driver's whole config surface is a bare struct of raw numbers (`baudrate: u32`, `clock_hz: Option<u32>`) whose validity is purely runtime and clock-dependent, and `configure_uart` only clamps the low end while leaving the high end (the dominant defect) totally unguarded, never enables the UART clock gate / pinmux, and mis-handles the 5-data-bit + 2-stop frame. The tightening folds the three coupled numeric defects (high-end div=0, low-end silent clamp, clock_hz/base mismatch) into ONE construct-time-validated newtype `BaudRate` whose fallible constructor `try_new(baud, pclk)` accepts iff `1 <= pclk/(16*baud) <= 65535` (i.e. `pclk/(16*65535)+1 <= baud <= pclk/16`), exactly mirroring the PWM `PwmPeriod::try_from_hz` pattern. Because the constructor must be given the *actual* pclk, the clock_hz mismatch becomes structurally hard to make: `UartClock` is a small enum (Pll160M / BootTcxo24M / BootTcxo40M / runtime probe) that yields the pclk fed to `BaudRate`, replacing the foot-gun `Option<u32>`. The class-C clock-gate/pinmux precondition is fixed at the driver level by an `enable_uart_clock(idx)` self-enable in `configure_uart` (CKEN_CTL1 bits 18/19/20 from clock.rs, plus the per-instance TXD/RXD pinmux), exactly the PWM "construct -> clocked" lesson. The 5-bit/2-stop frame collision is fixed by making `configure_uart` honor the v151 rule (stp=1 means 1.5 stops when dlen=0). All embedded-io / embedded-hal-nb / embedded-io-async trait impls are byte-for-byte untouched (they only call `write_byte`/`read_byte`, not config). OPEN QUESTION needing on-board measurement: (1) whether silicon actually runs at div=1 with a large div_fra or needs div>=2 (would raise the high-end ceiling predicate from `<= pclk/16` to `<= pclk/32`); (2) the BS21/BS2X UART_CLOCK_HZ is TODO/assumed 32 MHz (soc/bs21.rs:34-35) so every BS2X bound is provisional until confirmed; (3) whether UART1/UART2 gate+pinmux are truly off at app entry (flashboot only provisions the UART0 console), which sets how load-bearing the self-enable is.

designs.surface: Config.baudrate HIGH end (integer divider underflow to div=0 when baud > pclk/16) — the dominant defect
designs.validation_bounds: ACCEPT iff 1 <= floor(pclk/(16*baud)) <= 65535, equivalently pclk/(16*65535)+1 <= baud <= pclk/16. Verified numerically: at 160 MHz accept [153, 10_000_000]; at 40 MHz [39, 2_500_000]; at 32 MHz (BS21) [31, 2_000_000]; at 24 MHz [23, 1_500_000]. REJECT the exact audited div=0 cases: (40M, 3_000_000), (32M, 3_000_000), (160M, 20_000_000) all give div=0 -> None. The low-end (div>0xFFFF) reject also subsumes the old silent clamp (see the LOW-end row). NOTE the `<= pclk/16` ceiling assumes silicon tolerates div=1; if on-board test shows div>=2 is required, tighten the predicate to `div >= 2` (ceiling becomes pclk/32).
designs.embedded_hal_note: Pure inherent config type. embedded_io::{Read,Write}, embedded_hal_nb::serial::{Read,Write}, embedded_io_async::{Read,Write} are all untouched — they call write_byte/read_byte/flush_tx only, never the Config. No trait signature changes.

designs.surface: Config.clock_hz: Option<u32> (None silently selects 160 MHz PLL even when running on the 24/40 MHz flashboot TCXO base -> ~4x-wrong baud; also shifts the unguarded high-end ceiling)
designs.validation_bounds: No new numeric bound of its own — its whole purpose is to make the base EXPLICIT and to feed the single source-of-truth pclk into BaudRate::try_new so the guard and the programmed divider use the identical value. UartClock::hz() must equal the constants in soc/ws63.rs (160e6 / 24e6 / 40e6) and soc/bs21.rs (32e6, TODO-confirm). BootProbe == uart_boot_clock_hz() reading HW_CTL 0x4000_0014 bit0 (1->24M, 0->40M), already silicon-confirmed (40 MHz board reads 0x2 -> div=21,fra=44 at 115200).
designs.embedded_hal_note: Inherent config only. All embedded-io / embedded-hal-nb / embedded-io-async impls untouched.

designs.surface: UART clock gate CKEN_CTL1[20:18] + TXD/RXD pinmux — unenforced precondition for new_uart1 / new_uart2 (class C)
designs.validation_bounds: Gate bit = 18 + idx, asserted by clock.rs:64-66 (Uart0/1/2 -> CKEN_CTL1 18/19/20) and Peripheral::cken_info() (Some((1,18/19/20))). No accept/reject predicate — this is a precondition-establishment, not a value check. The CKEN_CTL1 absolute address (0x4400_1104) and the per-instance TXD/RXD pinmux register/field are the board-population details that need the on-board confirmation flagged in notes: whether UART1/UART2 are truly ungated+unmuxed at app entry (flashboot only provisions the UART0 console).
designs.embedded_hal_note: Internal to configure_uart; no public type or trait change. embedded-io/-nb/-async impls untouched. (UART0 keeps working because it is already clocked+muxed by flashboot; the self-enable is idempotent there.)

designs.surface: Config.baudrate LOW end (silent clamp to min_baud instead of signalling) — uart.rs:122-123
designs.validation_bounds: REJECT (None) iff div = pclk/(16*baud) > 65535, i.e. baud < pclk/(16*65535)+1. Exact lower bounds: 153 (160M), 39 (40M), 31 (32M), 23 (24M). A caller asking 50 baud at 160 MHz now gets None at construction instead of a silent ~153 baud. The 8-bit DIV_L/DIV_H and 6-bit DIV_FRA pre-mask in configure_uart is preserved as defense-in-depth but is now provably never load-bearing.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Same BaudRate newtype; trait impls untouched.

designs.surface: Config { data_bits: Five, stop_bits: Two } — stp=1 with dlen=0 yields 1.5 stop bits, not 2 (v151 frame mismatch)
designs.validation_bounds: Trigger predicate: data_bits == Five AND stop_bits == Two. Hardware fact (hal_uart_v151_regs_def.h:77-80, ws63-pac lib.rs:6679): stp bit7 = 1 means 1.5 stop bits when DLEN==0 (5 data bits), else 2 stop bits. The realized frame for Five+Two is 5-data/1.5-stop; documented and intentional rather than a silent surprise. No clock/divider interaction.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Internal to configure_uart + a doc note on the StopBits enum. No trait or signature change; embedded-io/-nb/-async untouched.

designs.surface: div_fra (6-bit fractional divider) and DataBits/Parity/StopBits encodings
designs.validation_bounds: None required. div_fra is always in 0..=63 by the `& 0x3F` mask and matches vendor exactly; dlen[3:2] only ever receives 0..3; parity/stop are single bits. No accept/reject predicate is meaningful.
designs.embedded_hal_note: N/A — nothing changes.

designs.surface: Operational write_byte/read_byte/write taking a runtime idx: u8 independent of the type-state instance (SCOPE-NOTE foot-gun, not a Config-value defect)
designs.validation_bounds: N/A for this audit (excluded by the scope note: not a constructible-but-unprogrammable Config value).
designs.embedded_hal_note: Touching this would change write_byte/read_byte inherent signatures (drop the idx param); deferred precisely to avoid churn to the operational surface the embedded-io/-nb/-async impls call. Left untouched.

designs.summary: The gadc driver's danger is concentrated in two unenforced-precondition (class C) surfaces and one fragile-default (class B) surface, plus a measured-width (class B) question on the result layout. Top priority is the UNBOUNDED done-poll in convert_once (gadc.rs:199-201): a fully-valid read(Ain0) can deadlock the core forever, and the vendor v154 pattern (`COUNT_THRESHOLD 1000` bounded loops, hal_adc_v154.c:33) is the exact fix — bound the loop and return Result<i32, GadcError>, making read() and a new try_read() fallible. Second, new() advances on bare delay_us() spins and never reads the RO ack bits it documents (pwr_ack PMU_AFE_DIG_PWR_EN[2], done PMU_AFE_GADC_CFG[4], gadc.rs:36-37); it should become `try_new() -> Result<Self, GadcError>` that polls those bits with the same bounded loop and self-enables/acks its own AFE bring-up (the class-C "construct -> live" guarantee, mirroring PWM's enable_pwm_clock). The delay_us 64 MHz assumption (gadc.rs:81) should route through the existing tcxo-based delay like the vendor's uapi_tcxo_delay_us instead of a CPU-cycle spin. cfg_clk_div_0=0x27 (prechg_div=0) becomes a validated GadcClkDiv newtype with a non-zero-prechg predicate. The 18-bit-signed-at-bit-17 result width and the exact PMU ack-bit registers are NOT verifiable in this checkout (fbb_bs2x v153 absent) and CANNOT be validated on the connected WS63 HIL rig — gadc is `#[cfg(feature="chip-bs21")]` (lib.rs:101) and the WS63 board runs chip-ws63, so every on-silicon claim here needs a BS2X board; host proptests can only validate the pure newtype/sign-extend logic. OPEN QUESTION needing on-board (BS2X) measurement: (a) does rpt_gadc_data_3 bit0 actually assert within COUNT_THRESHOLD iterations under a correct bring-up, (b) the true accumulator width / sign-bit position of rpt_gadc_data_2, (c) the COMMON_DEFAULT cfg_clk_div_0 value and whether prechg_div really must be non-zero, (d) the precise PMU ack-bit register offsets.

designs.surface: convert_once done-poll on rpt_gadc_data_3 bit0 (reached via read())
designs.validation_bounds: Accept (return Ok) iff rpt_gadc_data_3 bit0 (`bits() & 0x1`) becomes 1 within cnt in 0..=DONE_POLL_THRESHOLD (1000) poll iterations; on the 1001st failing read, re-isolate the AFE and return Err(GadcError::ConversionTimeout). Bound value 1000 is taken directly from the vendor `#define COUNT_THRESHOLD 1000` (hal_adc_v154.c:33) that bounds every status-wait in the authoritative driver. Predicate is iteration-count based (not time based) to stay clock-independent like the surrounding control path.
designs.embedded_hal_note: gadc exposes NO embedded-hal trait impls today (read() is an inherent method, not embedded_hal_nb adc::OneShot/voltage), so changing read()'s return type breaks no trait contract. If an embedded-hal-nb OneShot impl is added later it would wrap try_read and map GadcError into its own Error type via nb::Error — the trait Result idiom already accommodates this. No SetDutyCycle/SpiBus/I2c/Read/Write signatures exist here to touch.

designs.surface: new() power-up: poll PMU_AFE_DIG_PWR_EN pwr_ack (RO bit2) and PMU_AFE_GADC_CFG done (RO bit4) instead of bare delays; make construction fallible + self-enabling
designs.validation_bounds: Accept (Ok(Gadc)) iff PMU_AFE_DIG_PWR_EN & (1<<2) != 0 within <=1000 polls AND PMU_AFE_GADC_CFG & (1<<4) != 0 within <=1000 polls; otherwise Err(GadcError::PowerUpTimeout). Bit positions 2 (pwr_ack) and 4 (done) are the driver's own self-documented RO acks (gadc.rs:36-37); poll bound 1000 matches the vendor COUNT_THRESHOLD. The exact PMU ack-bit *register offsets* are confirmed only by the driver's comments, not by fbb_bs2x v153 (absent) — needs on-board confirmation that bit2/bit4 at these offsets are the live-handshake acks before relying on the poll target.
designs.embedded_hal_note: new()/try_new() is an inherent constructor, not part of any embedded-hal trait, so making it fallible touches no trait impl. Keep the old infallible `new()` deprecated-forwarding to `try_new().expect(...)` only if a non-breaking transition is wanted; the preferred 0.5.0 form is to replace new() with try_new(). The class-C self-enable (cfg_clken 0x0011_1111 written inside try_new) mirrors PWM's enable_pwm_clock so 'construct -> clocked' holds.

designs.surface: delay_us core-frequency assumption (us * 64) used for all analog settling + the convert trigger delays
designs.validation_bounds: No accept/reject predicate — this is a precondition that types cannot express (a settling delay has no representable invalid value). The guard is: route through the TCXO-based delay (fixed rate, vendor hal_adc_v154.c:64-69 DELAY_500US/DELAY_300US via tcxo) so the 30/50/150/5 us waits are independent of the app-core clock. Prior silicon finding (BS2X boot clock can be the TCXO, not the nominal 64 MHz core) is the direct evidence this matters. Doc the assumption explicitly where the spin remains.
designs.embedded_hal_note: delay_us is a private free function with no trait surface; switching its implementation to the tcxo delay is invisible to embedded-hal. The driver does not impl embedded_hal::delay::DelayNs, so no DelayNs contract is affected.

designs.surface: cfg_clk_div_0 = 0x27 magic (ana_div_th[11:0] / prechg_div_th[23:12], 12-bit each)
designs.validation_bounds: Accept iff ana_div <= 0x0FFF AND prechg_div <= 0x0FFF AND prechg_div != 0. Field widths 0:11 and 12:23 (12-bit each) are confirmed from the PAC (cfg_clk_div ana_div_th = Bits 0:11, prechg_div_th = Bits 12:23). The prechg_div != 0 rule encodes the audit's suspicion that the current 0x27 (prechg_div=0) is a non-running combo. CAVEAT: the true COMMON_DEFAULT value is not in this checkout; COMMON_DEFAULT is kept as 0x27 only to preserve current behavior and is flagged for on-board confirmation — note that 0x27 itself FAILS the prechg!=0 rule, which is exactly the signal that the in-source default is suspect and must be replaced with a board-measured value.
designs.embedded_hal_note: cfg_clk_div_0 is written only inside the inherent constructor (try_new), never via an embedded-hal trait. Adding GadcClkDiv as an internal validated constant (and optionally a try_new_with_clkdiv(div: GadcClkDiv) variant) touches no trait impl.

designs.surface: rpt_gadc_data_2 result: 18-bit sign-extend-at-bit-17 vs measured accumulator width
designs.validation_bounds: Current mask/sign math is correct FOR the assumed layout (low 18 bits significant, sign bit 17): accept any u32, mask to 0x3FFFF, treat bit17 as sign — exhaustively host-tested and bijective over 18 bits. There is NO new accept/reject predicate to add at the type level because the input is hardware-produced, not user-supplied. The guard is documentation + an explicit board-confirmation flag: the 18-bit/sign-bit-17 layout is the datasheet/v154-analog assumption, and per the PWM lesson the TYPE MUST ENCODE MEASURED SILICON. Bound to verify on board: actual significant-bit count, sign-bit index, and whether the v153 satrt_cnt equals v154's 0x18 (hal_adc_v154_regs_op.c:74).
designs.embedded_hal_note: The result path is an inherent read()/try_read() returning i32, not an embedded-hal adc trait, so the i32 representation is free to keep. If an embedded_hal_nb OneShot is added later its WORD type would be the measured-width integer; that is a future trait choice and is not constrained now.

designs.surface: absolute base addresses GADC_BASE / PMU_AFE_BASE / AON_AFE_ISO + chip gating of the whole module
designs.validation_bounds: No value predicate — this is a compile-time chip precondition, not a runtime range. The guard: the module is gated `#[cfg(feature = "chip-bs21")]` at lib.rs:101 (already present, contrary to the audit's 'no cfg gate' claim, which was scoped to gadc.rs itself), so the raw BS2X addresses (0x5703_6000 / 0x5700_8700 / 0x5702_C230) cannot be compiled for chip-ws63. Add an in-file `#[cfg(not(feature="chip-bs21"))] compile_error!` so direct inclusion also fails loudly. WS63's ADC is the separate lsadc at 0x4400_C000 (adc_porting.c:21), reached through its own gated module — no address can be mis-targeted across chips.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Pure cfg/documentation change; no trait surface involved. Does not alter any SetDutyCycle/SpiBus/I2c/Read/Write impl (gadc has none).

designs.summary: The I2S config surface is the worst-affected driver in the audit: the ws63-pac SVD encodes a fabricated textbook layout for the `mode` and `i2s_crg` registers that contradicts the authoritative vendor `hal_sio_v151_regs_def.h`, so essentially NO structurally-valid `Master` config is runnable today (the master/slave-select bit7 is never written, and the crg clock-enables at bit0/bit1 are never set — `configure` writes 0x100/bit8, a reserved bit). The tightening therefore has two halves that must land together: (1) regenerate the PAC `mode`/`i2s_crg` from the vendor regs_def, then drive the correct bits, and (2) wrap every divider/threshold field in a construct-time-validated newtype and make a zero-divider Master unrepresentable via a type-state `Master`/`Slave` split (mirroring PWM's `PwmPeriod`/`Duty` and the role-as-type pattern). The driver must also self-enable its own clock tree in `configure` (CMU_NEW_CFG0 bit0 + CKEN_CTL0 bit11 bus + bit12 clk), exactly as PWM self-enables CLK_SEL/CKEN/DIV — that is the class-C gate. ChannelCount and DataWidth enums must be re-tabled to the silicon enums (no 6ch; data widths are 16/18/20/24/32-bit with code0 reserved, not 8/10/12/14/16/18/20/24). Newtype range checks, the clock-gate self-enable, and post-configure register read-back are validatable on the connected board at register/poll level (the existing `i2s_version_live` HIL test already proves the window is live and can be extended to read back `mode`/`i2s_crg`/dividers). OPEN QUESTIONS needing on-board measurement (cannot be settled from headers): (a) whether DataWidth code5 (32-bit) actually latches on this part and which of 16/18/20/24 the codec path accepts — parallel to PWM's pwm_freq-high-half lesson; (b) whether BOTH CKEN bit11+bit12 AND CMU_NEW_CFG0 bit0 are required for the SIO block to respond (sio_porting sets all three); (c) effective FIFO depth for threshold bounding (vendor SIO_FIFO_SIZE=8); (d) whether i2s_fs_sel/i2s_bclk_sel (crg b2/b4) must be explicitly cleared. Producing an actual BCLK/FS waveform or audio frame is NOT board-validatable here (no scope/codec/jumper for I2S).

designs.surface: I2sConfig.role + bclk_div/fs_div_num/fs_div_ratio (Master with all dividers=0)
designs.validation_bounds: ACCEPT: role=Master only when constructed with BclkDiv/FsDivNum/FsDivRatio newtypes (each of which independently rejects an all-zero/out-of-range divider at construction). REJECT (at compile time, not runtime): a Master with no dividers — the struct simply cannot be built. Slave needs no dividers. The canonical broken case `role=Master, bclk_div=0, fs_div_num=0, fs_div_ratio=0` becomes uninstantiable. Note the vendor never lets the user pass raw dividers at all (it computes fs_div_num=data_width*channels, fs_div_ratio=that/2, bclk via sio_porting_get_bclk_div_num); a future ergonomic constructor `master_from_audio(sample_rate, data_width, channels)` can compute these and is the recommended default path, but the newtype floor guarantees safety even for the raw constructor.
designs.embedded_hal_note: I2S has no embedded-hal trait surface today (no embedded-hal-audio trait exists in 1.0); configure() is an inherent method, so making it generic over Role is free and touches no trait impl. The operational read/write (write_left/right u32, read_left/right) are inherent and unchanged.

designs.surface: I2sConfig (Default impl that ships role=Master, all dividers=0)
designs.validation_bounds: REJECT by construction: there is no way to obtain a Master config without supplying validated dividers. A `Default` that yielded a zero-divider Master (today's i2s.rs:107-124) is deleted. ACCEPT: `I2sConfig::<Slave>::default()` (no clocks generated locally, so zero dividers are correct and harmless).
designs.embedded_hal_note: Default is a std/core trait, not embedded-hal; removing the broken Default impl does not affect any embedded-hal trait bound.

designs.surface: configure(): i2s_crg clock-enable write (currently 0x100 = bit8, a reserved bit)
designs.validation_bounds: ACCEPT/produce-clock predicate: after configure(role=Master), i2s_crg read-back must have bit0(bclk_div_en)=1 AND bit1(crg_clken)=1, bit8=0 (no longer touched). REJECT the old behavior: writing 0x100 (bit8) which set a RESERVED bit and left bits0/1 clear so the BCLK divider was never enabled. Bound source: hal_sio_v151_regs_def.h:385-395, hal_i2s_set_bclk drives bclk_div_en.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Driver-internal; no public type or trait signature changes. No embedded-hal impl involved.

designs.surface: configure(): mode register bit packing (channels@0:1, clk_edge@2, master@3, pcm@4)
designs.validation_bounds: ACCEPT/correct predicate: 'Master' sets bit7 (ms_mode_sel), NOT bit3; channel code lands in bits[4:5], NOT bits[0:1]; clk_edge lands on bit6, NOT bit2; pcm lands on bit0, NOT bit4. REJECT today's packing where Rust 'Master'(bit3) actually set ext_rec_en, ms_mode_sel(bit7) was never written so the part stayed a slave, and channels in bits[0:1] corrupted the i2s/pcm select. Bound source: hal_sio_v151_regs_def.h:47-71; contradicts ws63-pac lib.rs:20181-20239.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Driver-internal re-pack; no public type changes beyond the enums below and no embedded-hal trait involved.

designs.surface: ChannelCount enum (Two=0,Four=1,Six=2,Eight=3)
designs.validation_bounds: ACCEPT exactly the four silicon codes: Two->0, Four->1, Eight->2, Sixteen->3. REJECT (by removal) the fabricated Six. Fixes the silent mis-mapping where ChannelCount::Six(code2) selected 8ch and ChannelCount::Eight(code3) selected 16ch. Bound source: hal_sio_v151_regs_def.h:60-64 (chn_num 00=2/01=4/10=8/11=16), hal_sio.h:169-178 (TWO_CH=0..SIXTEEN_CH=3). PAC doc '2=6ch;3=8ch' is also wrong and is corrected.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Pure inherent-config enum; not referenced by any embedded-hal trait. No trait impl affected.

designs.surface: DataWidth enum (Bits8=0,Bits10,Bits12,Bits14,Bits16=4,Bits18,Bits20,Bits24=7)
designs.validation_bounds: ACCEPT exactly codes 1..=5 (16/18/20/24/32-bit) — code0 RESERVED is unrepresentable (no variant). REJECT the fabricated 8/10/12/14-bit codes (do not exist) AND the old default Bits16=4 which actually programmed 24-bit, AND Bits24=7 which was outside the defined 0..5 set. Bound source: hal_sio.h:147-160 (RESERVED=0..THIRTY_TWO_BIT=5), regs_def data_width_set tx/rx 3 bits each (PAC TxModeW/RxModeW width 3, lib.rs:20791/20795). Whether 32-bit(code5) physically latches is the PWM-style open question and gets a doc note + HIL read-back, not a type that asserts it works.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Pure inherent-config enum; no embedded-hal trait references it. No trait impl affected.

designs.surface: bclk_div: u8 written as `bits & 0x7F` (silent truncation of 128..=255)
designs.validation_bounds: ACCEPT 0..=127; REJECT 128..=255 (returns None at construction instead of wrapping modulo 128 — e.g. today bclk_div=200 silently programs 72). Bound source: hal_sio_v151_regs_def.h:402-408 (bclk_div_num:7), PAC BclkDivNumW FieldWriter width 7 (lib.rs:21039, confirmed in-tree). Note: a non-zero divider is what the Master type-state requires; from_count(0) is allowed by the newtype itself but the Master ergonomic constructor / vendor formula never yields 0 — if a hard non-zero floor is wanted, change predicate to `n>=1 && n<=127`.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Config-only newtype, not used by any embedded-hal trait method. No trait impl changes.

designs.surface: fs_div_num: u16 written as `bits & 0x3FF` (silent truncation of 1024..=65535)
designs.validation_bounds: ACCEPT 0..=1023; REJECT 1024..=65535 (None at construction instead of &0x3FF truncation). Bound source: hal_sio_v151_regs_def.h:415-421 (fs_div_num:10); PAC FsDivNumW FieldWriter width 10 (lib.rs ~21080, confirmed width-10 in-tree).
designs.embedded_hal_note: Config-only newtype; no embedded-hal trait references it. No trait impl changes.

designs.surface: fs_div_ratio: u16 written as `bits & 0x7FF` (silent truncation of 2048..=65535)
designs.validation_bounds: ACCEPT 0..=2047; REJECT 2048..=65535 (None at construction instead of &0x7FF truncation). Bound source: hal_sio_v151_regs_def.h:428-434 (fs_div_ratio_num:11); PAC FsDivRatioNumW FieldWriter width 11 (lib.rs ~21121, confirmed width-11 in-tree).
designs.embedded_hal_note: Config-only newtype; no embedded-hal trait references it. No trait impl changes.

designs.surface: I2S clock gate: CKEN_CTL0 bit11(bus)+bit12(clk) and CMU_NEW_CFG0 bit0 (class C — no self-enable)
designs.validation_bounds: ENABLE predicate: before writing any SIO register, configure() must set CKEN_CTL0 bit11 (bus) and bit12 (clk) and CMU_NEW_CFG0 bit0; without them the entire SIO MMIO block is unclocked and reads/writes are no-ops. Bound source: sio_porting.c:85-96 (clock_enable sets CMU_NEW_CFG0 bit0, 0x44001100 bit12 I2S_CLKEN, bit11 I2S_BUS_CLKEN); HAL clock.rs:63 already records Peripheral::I2s=>Some((0,12)) clk + bit11 bus. OPEN: confirm on-board whether all three bits are required (summary open-Q b) — hence doc-and-guard rather than a type.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Driver-internal clock bring-up; no public type or embedded-hal trait signature change (same shape as PWM's in-configure clock enable).

designs.surface: tx_fifo_threshold / rx_fifo_threshold: u8 (0..=255) vs 8-entry FIFO
designs.validation_bounds: ACCEPT 0..=8 (FIFO depth); REJECT 9..=255 (None at construction — those thresholds can never be reached so the threshold interrupt silently never fires). Replaces today's u8 field that accepts the full 8-bit range and the broken Default of 8 (== full depth, vendor uses rx=7/tx=4). Bound source: hal_sio_v151.c:19-21 (SIO_FIFO_SIZE 8, SIO_RX_THRESHOLD 7, SIO_TX_THRESHOLD 4); fifo_threshold fields 8-bit each (regs_def 233-240 / PAC 20656-20679). DEPTH itself flagged for on-board confirmation.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Config-only newtype; no embedded-hal trait references the FIFO thresholds. No trait impl changes.

designs.surface: loopback: bool (blind write of 1<<8 to the version register, no RMW, no txrx-enable)
designs.validation_bounds: ACCEPT loopback=true only as a RMW set of version bit8 (the loop bit position itself is correct); the predicate the type CANNOT express is the required pairing with hal_sio_v151_txrx_enable + crg clock toggle — that is a multi-register operational sequence, hence a doc-and-guard note rather than a new type. REJECT today's blind `version().write(1<<8)` which clobbers the read-only IP-version bits and omits the enable sequence. Bound source: hal_sio_v151_regs_def.h:31-40 (version[0:7]+loop@b8), hal_sio_v151.c:342-403 (loop is RMW + needs txrx_enable/crg clock). Lower severity: bit position matches, only self-sufficiency is the gap.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Driver-internal; `loopback` stays a plain bool config field, no embedded-hal trait involved.

designs.summary: The lsadc (v154, base 0x4400_C000) tightening splits cleanly: three class-A silent-truncation fields (sample_cnt 5b, cast_cnt 7b, rxintsize 3b) become construct-time-validated newtypes returning Option, mirroring PwmPeriod/Duty — once constructed they are guaranteed programmable, so the driver drops its `& 0x1F`/`& 0x7F`/`& 0x07` masks entirely (those masks are redundant anyway: the PAC FieldWriter::bits already masks `& mask::<WI>()`, confirmed at lib.rs:384-387, so the existing code truncates twice and signals never). The load-bearing class-C gap is that LsAdc, unlike PWM, never brings up its own gate: it must self-enable the CLDO bus clock + deassert soft_rst_lsadc_n(bit5)/soft_rst_lsadc_bus_n(bit7) at cldo_crg_rst_soft_cfg_1 @ 0x4400_1138 in new(), using exactly the raw-MMIO rmw pattern the sibling gadc driver already uses (gadc.rs:57-85) and PWM uses (pwm.rs:120-149); without it no config write latches. The AFE staged power-up (da_lsadc_en |= 0x7000 -> 0xE7F -> 0x100 -> 0x80 with 500us/300us tcxo delays, plus simu_cfg writes to da_lsadc_rwreg_1..3 which are NOT in the PAC RegisterBlock — confirmed absent) plus offset/gain/cap calibration must move into a single staged power_up() method; the raw set_analog_enable overwrite is removed (class B). CIC/offset/gain stay no-change-but-deprecated (vendor never writes them; dead in the supported raw path). channel pinmux (class C) and the unbounded conversion wait (class C, vendor caps at COUNT_THRESHOLD=1000) get a guard method + a Result-returning timeout. OPEN QUESTION needing on-board measurement: per the PWM high-half-doesn't-latch precedent, whether the full PAC widths (cic_osr 8b, da_lsadc_en 16b) and even the staged AFE ramp actually produce a live conversion on silicon is unconfirmed — the current HIL rig (tests/hil.rs) has no lsadc coverage and no analog source, so only the newtype constructors and register-latch (post clock-gate) are board-validatable at register-poll level; an actual conversion-correct test needs an analog stimulus part.

designs.surface: configure_scan — AdcConfig.sample_count (sample_cnt, 5-bit)
designs.validation_bounds: accept count <= 31, reject count >= 32. Bound = (1<<5)-1 = 31, from PAC SampleCntW=FieldWriter<5> (lib.rs:21283) and vendor adc_ctrl_data.sample_cnt:5 (regs_def.h:158) — confirmed against BOTH PAC width and vendor bitfield, so it is a real register cap not datasheet-only.
designs.embedded_hal_note: configure_scan / AdcConfig are inherent (non-trait) config surfaces, free to type. No embedded-hal trait touches AdcConfig; LsAdc exposes no embedded-hal trait for scan timing, so nothing breaks.

designs.surface: configure_scan — AdcConfig.cast_count (cast_cnt, 7-bit)
designs.validation_bounds: accept count <= 127, reject count >= 128. Bound = (1<<7)-1 = 127, from PAC CastCntW=FieldWriter<7> (lib.rs:21291) and vendor adc_ctrl_data.cast_cnt:7 (regs_def.h:160).
designs.embedded_hal_note: Inherent config field on AdcConfig; no embedded-hal trait involved. Untouched: nothing — LsAdc implements no embedded-hal ADC trait on this path.

designs.surface: set_fifo_waterline — level (rxintsize, 3-bit)
designs.validation_bounds: accept level <= 7, reject level >= 8. Bound = (1<<3)-1 = 7, from PAC RxintsizeW=FieldWriter<3> (lib.rs:21372) and vendor adc_fifo_data.rxintsize:3 (regs_def.h:132). SDK default 0x6 fits.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Inherent method, not embedded-hal. The async ConvFuture / IRQ path is unaffected by the argument-type change.

designs.surface: new / enable / configure_scan / start_scan — CLDO bus clock + soft-reset gate (cldo_crg_rst_soft_cfg_1 @ 0x4400_1138, soft_rst_lsadc_n bit5 / soft_rst_lsadc_bus_n bit7)
designs.validation_bounds: Gate predicate: after new(), cldo_crg_rst_soft_cfg_1 bit5 AND bit7 == 1 (active-low deasserted). Vendor hal_adc_soft_rst toggles exactly these two bits at 0x44001138 (adc_porting.c:22; regs_op.h:57-64; hal_adc_v154.c:184-185); adc_port_clock_enable/init_clock are empty stubs (adc_porting.c:39-47) so this toggle is the only gate. Types cannot express a cross-peripheral MMIO precondition; this is the self-enable-clock driver change, not a typed surface.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait involved; new() is a constructor. All existing trait impls (none on this path) untouched. This is the load-bearing class-C fix that makes every typed config write above actually latch.

designs.surface: configure_scan / start_scan / read_sample / read_async — AFE analog power-up (da_lsadc_rwreg_1..3 simu writes + staged da_lsadc_en ramp + tcxo delays + offset/cap/gain cal)
designs.validation_bounds: Predicate is the timed staged sequence itself: rwreg_1..3 = 0x155240/0x5/0x100; then da_lsadc_en accumulates 0x7000 |(500us) 0xE7F |(300us) 0x100 | 0x80; then calibration; then rstn release. From hal_adc_simulation_cfg (hal_adc_v154.c:58-71) + regs_op.h:66-124 + adc_process_before_use_adc (hal_adc_v154.c:152-192). No scalar bound to type — it is an ordered-with-delays precondition, hence doc-and-guard (a method that enforces the order), not a newtype. OPEN: whether this ramp yields a live conversion on silicon is unconfirmed (PWM high-half precedent).
designs.embedded_hal_note: Inherent method; no embedded-hal trait. read_sample/read_async signatures unchanged. power_up() becomes the documented precondition for any read.

designs.surface: set_analog_enable — bits (da_lsadc_en, 16-bit, overwrite semantics)
designs.validation_bounds: No accept/reject scalar — the defect is overwrite-vs-read-modify-OR + missing timed delays, not width (u16 into 16-bit, no truncation). Correct semantics enforced by the staged ramp in power_up (regs_op.h:94-124; hal_adc_v154.c:58-71). PAC DaLsadcEnW=FieldWriter<16,u16> (lib.rs:21776). The 16-bit-may-not-all-latch question carries the PWM caveat -> needs board.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait. Removing/deprecating an inherent method; nothing in the trait surface changes.

designs.surface: enable_cic_filter — oversampling_ratio (cic_osr 8-bit) + cic_filter_en
designs.validation_bounds: Only firm reject: osr == 0 (zero OSR -> undefined decimation -> no usable output). No upper bound can be derived: grep of vendor drivers/ finds zero cic_osr writes (only regs_def.h:85-86 declaration) so no min/typ OSR is documented. PAC CicOsrW=FieldWriter<8> (lib.rs:22036), CicFilterEnW=BitWriter (lib.rs:21995). Cannot newtype a range with no known bound -> guard the osr=0 floor + the data_sel coupling, deprecate.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Inherent method, no embedded-hal trait. Adding a Result return is a non-trait signature change.

designs.surface: set_offset / set_gain — offset (cfg_offset 16-bit) / gain (cfg_gain 16-bit)
designs.validation_bounds: No accept/reject predicate: full-width u16 into 16-bit fields, no truncation possible. The defect is no-observable-effect in the raw path, not range. Evidence: SDK writes cfg_adc_offset_cali/_en, cfg_gain_cali_en (regs_op.h:156-281; hal_adc_v154.c:73-178), never cfg_offset/cfg_gain (grep: none). PAC OffsetW/GainW=FieldWriter<16,u16> (lib.rs:21913,21954). Whether all 16 bits latch carries the PWM caveat.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Inherent methods; no embedded-hal trait. Deprecation only; signatures unchanged, so non-breaking aside from the lint.

designs.surface: configure_scan — channel enable bitmask (implied GPIO pad provisioning, GPIO_07..GPIO_12 for ch0..5)
designs.validation_bounds: Predicate: for ch in 0..=5, pin = 7+ch is configured pull=DISABLE, mode=PIN_MODE_0, dir=INPUT. From hal_adc_v154_channel_set mapping ADC_CHANNEL_0..5 -> GPIO_07..GPIO_12 with uapi_pin_set_pull(DISABLE)+uapi_pin_set_mode(PIN_MODE_0)+uapi_gpio_set_dir(INPUT) (hal_adc_v154.c:194-238). This is a board/pad precondition (which physical pad carries analog), expressible as a guard inside configure_scan but not as a value type.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Inherent configure_scan; no embedded-hal trait. Reuses the existing io_config/gpio HAL with no trait changes.

designs.surface: read_async / data_ready — conversion-complete wait (no timeout)
designs.validation_bounds: accept: data_ready() true within 1000 poll iterations; reject (Err::Timeout): exceeds 1000. Bound = COUNT_THRESHOLD = 1000 from hal_adc_v154.c:33, used at :80-85,108-113,125-130,143-148,164-177,329-339. Current Rust ConvFuture (lsadc.rs:324-351) and data_ready() (lsadc.rs:175-177) have no bound. Result is the idiomatic fallible signal for the unmet-precondition case.
designs.embedded_hal_note: These are bespoke inherent async/poll methods (LSADC has no embedded-hal ADC trait; embedded-hal 1.0 has no blocking ADC trait). Changing read_async to return Result is a non-trait change; no embedded-hal impl exists to break. data_ready() stays bool (unchanged); the new read_sample_timeout is additive.

designs.driver: timer (hisi-riscv-hal: src/timer.rs, src/time.rs) — WS63 / BS2X TIMER_V150
designs.summary: The timer driver's config surface is tightened with one construct-time-validated newtype, TimerTicks(u32), mirroring PWM's Duty/PwmPeriod pattern: it has from_ticks (rejects 0), try_from_micros / try_from_millis / try_from_nanos (reject 0 and >max, returning Option instead of the current silent clamp/truncate), and a max_micros() bound derived from TIMER_CLOCK_HZ. configure()/start()/start_micros()/start_millis() are retyped to take TimerTicks, so the class-B zero-period and class-D over-range/sub-tick defects become unrepresentable rather than silently wrong. The control-register write in configure() is changed from a blind full w.bits() to a read-modify-write that preserves int_mask (PAC bit 3), fixing the class-C implicit-unmask side-effect using named PAC fields. The class-C wrong-crystal-frequency defect is the highest-severity item but CANNOT be fixed by a type alone: TIMER_CLOCK_HZ is a compile-time constant while the WS63 timer counts at a runtime-selected 24-or-40 MHz crystal; the design adds a runtime TimerClock read of the HW_CTL TCXO strap (reusing the proven uart_boot_clock_hz() pattern) so the us/ms conversions use the measured crystal, plus a doc-and-guard board precondition. The class-B HW-periodic-mode item stays no-change in code but is flagged doc-and-guard pending on-board confirmation (the vendor never programs HW mode=1). Open questions needing on-board measurement: (a) does HW mode=1 actually auto-reload on WS63 silicon, since the vendor forces one-shot + software reload; (b) the exact target-board WS63 crystal (24 vs 40 MHz) — the HW_CTL strap read must be scope-confirmed against produced delays; (c) BS21's actual TCXO (32 MHz is self-flagged TODO). The HIL suite can validate the ticks/zero/over-range typing and the int_mask-preserving RMW at the register/poll level; the crystal-frequency correctness needs a scope on the timer output.

designs.surface: OneShotTimer::start_micros/start_millis, PeriodicTimer::start_micros (us/ms -> ticks; silent clamp to u32::MAX past ~178.96 s @24 MHz)
designs.validation_bounds: Accept us in 1..=max_micros() where max_micros() = u32::MAX/(TIMER_CLOCK_HZ/1e6) = 178_956_970 us (~178.96 s) at 24 MHz, 107_374_182 us (~107.37 s) at 40 MHz. Reject us==0 (-> ticks==0) and us>max_micros() (-> ticks>u32::MAX). ms path: accept 1..=178_956 ms @24 MHz, reject 0 and >that. The u64 intermediate never overflows (24e6*4.29e9 ~ 1e17 < u64::MAX), so the only change is None-instead-of-clamp at the u32::MAX boundary. Mirrors vendor timer.c:425 (time_us > uapi_timer_get_max_us -> ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAM) and timer.c:416-419.
designs.embedded_hal_note: embedded_hal::delay::DelayNs (delay_us/delay_ms/delay_ns) on OneShotTimer is UNTOUCHED — its u32+()-return signatures are fixed by the trait. The DelayNs impl keeps its own internal saturating clamp (a delay that is too long is the trait's allowed best-effort, not an error channel), but is rewritten to route through TimerTicks::try_from_micros(us).unwrap_or(TimerTicks(u32::MAX-checked max)) so blocking and async agree. Only the inherent start_micros/start_millis (non-trait) gain the typed/fallible form.

designs.surface: TimerDriver::configure(load_value) / OneShotTimer::start(count) / PeriodicTimer::start(period) — load_value == 0
designs.validation_bounds: Reject load_value == 0 at construction (TimerTicks::from_ticks(0) -> None; try_from_micros/millis(0) -> None). Accept 1..=u32::MAX (full-width LoadCountW<32,u32>, ws63-pac lib.rs:23437; CONFIG_TIMER_0_WIDTH_64=0 so no class-A truncation). Matches vendor zero-reject timer.c:425 (time_us == 0 -> ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAM).
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait takes a raw load count — configure/start are inherent methods, free to retype. DelayNs is unaffected because it never passes 0 through start() once it routes via TimerTicks (a sub-tick or zero ns request becomes None and the trait method returns a best-effort, see ns row). embassy-time Driver (alarm on ch0) keeps working: it computes a non-zero tick count and now wraps it in TimerTicks::from_ticks(..).unwrap_or(TimerTicks(1)).

designs.surface: OneShotTimer::DelayNs::delay_ns and AsyncDelay::delay_ns (ns -> ticks; sub-tick truncates to 0 -> blocking no-op vs async max(1))
designs.validation_bounds: One tick = 1/TIMER_CLOCK_HZ = ~41.7 ns @24 MHz (~25 ns @40 MHz). try_from_nanos rejects ns whose product floors to 0 ticks (ns < ~41.7 ns @24 MHz) and ns producing > u32::MAX ticks. For the embedded-hal trait the chosen no-error policy is round-up-to-1-tick (delay >= requested, never the old silent zero-length no-op), unifying blocking with the async ticks.max(1). The fallible try_from_nanos is exposed as an inherent constructor for callers that want to detect sub-tick truncation.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Both embedded_hal::delay::DelayNs (blocking) and embedded_hal_async::delay::DelayNs (AsyncDelay) signatures stay exactly as-is (fn delay_ns(&mut self, u32)). The trait cannot return an error, so the fix is to make the two impls AGREE (round up to one tick) and stop the blocking no-op — a behaviour fix, not a signature change. Inherent fallible constructor lives outside the trait.

designs.surface: TimerDriver::configure / all start_*/delay_* — TIMER_CLOCK_HZ assumed 24 MHz (WS63) / 32 MHz (BS21), but WS63 crystal is runtime 24-or-40 MHz
designs.validation_bounds: Crystal must equal the const TIMER_CLOCK_HZ for the const-fn conversions to be physically correct: WS63 const = 24_000_000 (TCXO_HZ, soc/ws63.rs:48/56) — valid ONLY on CLK24M_TCXO boards; a CLK40M_TCXO board reads HW_CTL bit0 == 0 and counts at 40 MHz, making every const-based delay 40/24 = 1.667x too long and shifting max_micros from 178_956_970 to 107_374_182. The runtime timer_clock_hz() resolves 24 MHz (bit0==1) vs 40 MHz (bit0==0). BS21 TIMER_CLOCK_HZ = 32_000_000 is self-flagged TODO/unconfirmed (soc/bs21.rs:28-32) and needs an on-board measurement before any bound is trusted.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No trait change. embedded-hal DelayNs continues to use the compile-time const internally for its no-arg conversion (the trait has no clock parameter), so its accuracy still rides on the 24 MHz board precondition — documented as a guard. Direct/typed users who know they are on a 40 MHz board use the *_at(clock_hz) runtime constructors. Class-C note: unlike PWM there is NO per-peripheral clock GATE to self-enable (timer counts off the always-on TCXO the bootloader brought up), so there is no 'configure self-enables its gate' change here — the class-C exposure is purely the wrong FREQUENCY constant, addressed by the runtime read + guard.

designs.surface: TimerDriver::configure(mode, load_value) — blind full control write zeroes int_mask (bit 3), silently UNMASKING the IRQ each configure
designs.validation_bounds: mode field is bits 1:2 (2-bit, ModeW<...,2>); TimerMode encodes only 0 (OneShot) / 1 (Periodic), so (mode as u32)&0x3 cannot overflow into adjacent bits — no class-A truncation. The defect is the side-effect: the full write forces int_mask (bit 3) = 0 = UNMASKED every configure. RMW via .modify() preserves int_mask's prior value; default after reset is 0 (unmasked) which the async path relies on, but a caller that masked it keeps it masked. Mirrors vendor RMW which never clobbers int_mask.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait touches the control register — configure is inherent. DelayNs/AsyncDelay are unaffected functionally (async still gets an unmasked line because reset default is unmasked and it enables the IRQ itself). The change is purely that re-configuring a channel no longer silently re-unmasks a line the caller deliberately masked.

designs.surface: TimerDriver::configure(mode: TimerMode::Periodic) / PeriodicTimer::start — HW mode=1 auto-reload unverified on silicon (vendor never programs it)
designs.validation_bounds: No numeric bound — this is a behavioural precondition, not a value range. Accept: mode=1 IS what the driver intends to program (structurally valid, the 2-bit field holds 1 fine). Guard: the HARDWARE auto-reload behaviour for mode=1 on WS63 silicon is UNVERIFIED (vendor forces one-shot + software periodic, hal_timer_v150.c:78-87, driver/timer.c:170-185). A type cannot express 'this register value behaves as documented on silicon' — only a doc-and-guard + an on-board test can.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait involved (PeriodicTimer exposes inherent start/wait_tick, not a HAL trait). No signature change. The guard is documentation only, so it is fully embedded-hal-neutral.

designs.surface: configure_channel(transfer_size: u16) — the 12-bit trans_size field (dma.rs:272, control |= (transfer_size as u32) & 0xFFF)
designs.validation_bounds: Accept beats in 1..=4095; reject 0 (programs a zero-beat no-op that completes instantly copying nothing) and reject >=4096 (wraps: 4096->0, 5000->904). Bound 4095 = 2^12 - 1 from PAC DmacTransSize0W = FieldWriter<'a, REG, 12, u16> (lib.rs:15745) and vendor HAL_DMA_CH_MAX_TRANSFER_NUM=4096 reject (hal_dmac_v151.c:299-301). Lower bound 1 because a 0-beat transfer is a silent no-op.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal impact: configure_channel is an inherent driver method, not a trait method. DMA exposes no embedded-hal trait surface (no SpiBus/I2c/Read/Write here), so all trait impls — there are none on DmaDriver — stay untouched.

designs.surface: configure_channel(src_addr / dst_addr) cache-coherence precondition — driver kicks transfer (dma.rs:322-325) without any D-cache maintenance on a non-coherent cache
designs.validation_bounds: Not a value-range predicate; a precondition. Maintenance covers transfer_size.count() * width_bytes(src_width) bytes, rounded out to whole 32-byte CACHE_LINE in cache::maintain (cache.rs:34-35). clean done by driver when src_inc==true (incrementing RAM source); invalidate of the destination remains the caller's post-completion duty because the driver cannot know when the async transfer finishes. Evidence: non-coherent 4KB/32B-line cache (cache.rs:3-5); vendor flushes whole D-cache before ch_en (hal_dmac_v151.c:165-170).
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal impact (inherent method, no trait surface on DmaDriver). The existing cache::clean_range/invalidate_range/flush_range stay as-is — the change is the driver now calling clean itself, like PWM self-enabling its clock.

designs.surface: DmaChannelConfig.src_peripheral / dst_peripheral: public raw u8 (dma.rs:133-136); masked & 0x0F into the 4-bit src_per/dest_per field (dma.rs:297-298)
designs.validation_bounds: Accept only the DmaPeripheral discriminants {0,1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8,11,12,13,14} — all <= 0x0F by construction, so every constructible value fits the 4-bit field with no truncation. Reject (make unrepresentable) raw u8 >= 16 which previously wrapped to a wrong/no handshake line (16->Tie0, 17->Uart0Tx). The higher vendor MDMA handshaking IDs (16..28: SPI_M/OPI/QSPI1-3/SPI3/SPI4, dma_porting.h:56-84) exceed the 4-bit field and are genuinely unreachable, so the enum correctly omits them. Evidence: regs_def src_per:4/dest_per:4 (regs_def.h:169-170); driver mask (dma.rs:297-298).
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal impact (DmaChannelConfig is a plain config struct, no trait). mem_to_peripheral/peripheral_to_mem builders keep working (they now set Some(peri) instead of peri.request_id()).

designs.surface: Dma0::CLK_AUTO_CTRL = Some((0x4400_0244, 0x0008_0000)) hardcoded WS63 glue register, not chip-gated (dma.rs:60-63)
designs.validation_bounds: Not a value range; a per-chip address selection. On chip-ws63 keep (0x4400_0244, bit 19) — verified against vendor dma_porting.c and silicon (dma_mem_to_mem HIL passes only with this bypass). On chip-bs2x set None (do not write a WS63-specific address into an unknown BS2X register) until an on-board BS2X measurement provides the real offset/mask. Evidence: dma.rs:60-63; dma_porting.c:28-36; no BS2X equivalent address in tree (audit UNCERTAIN item 1).
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal impact (associated const on an internal instance trait). enable_controller's None-branch already exists, so no operational-surface change.

designs.surface: configure_channel / enable_channel(channel: u8) — physical_channel_index assert!()-panics for channel outside [base, base+4) (dma.rs:78-81)
designs.validation_bounds: Existing guard: accept channel in [CHANNEL_BASE, CHANNEL_BASE+4) = MDMA 0..=3, SDMA 8..=11; panic otherwise. Bound 4 = B_DMA_CHANNEL_MAX_NUM (dma_porting.h:30, hal_dmac_v151.c:65). Already signals (no silent wrap), so no truncation to prevent.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal impact (inherent methods). Leaving the u8 signatures unchanged keeps every existing caller and the HIL test (configure_channel(0, ...)) source-compatible.

designs.summary: The DMA driver needs four real tightenings plus one no-change. (A, breaking) transfer_size: replace the raw u16 + `& 0xFFF` truncation (dma.rs:272) with a TransferCount(u16) newtype, from_count -> Option valid only for 1..=4095 (12-bit field per PAC DmacTransSize0W lib.rs:15745, vendor reject at 4096); existence of the value means it programs without wrapping, and the mask is dropped. (A, breaking) src/dst_peripheral: drop the public raw u8 fields (dma.rs:133-136) in favour of the existing DmaPeripheral enum (Option<DmaPeripheral>, None=>Tie0), whose discriminants {0..14} all fit the 4-bit field, eliminating the `& 0x0F` wrap; higher vendor IDs (16..28) are genuinely unreachable so the enum rightly omits them. (C, non-breaking) cache coherence: make configure_channel self-service like PWM self-enabling its clock — clean_range the incrementing RAM source before the kick (mirroring vendor osal_dcache_flush_all before ch_en) and document that the caller must invalidate_range the destination after completion; addresses stay raw u32 because a type cannot tell a RAM buffer from a peripheral FIFO. (C, non-breaking) CLK_AUTO_CTRL: gate the 0x4400_0244/bit19 constant behind chip-ws63 and set None on chip-bs2x rather than poking a WS63-specific glue register on BS2X. (D, no-change) channel u8: the assert already signals (panics, not truncates), so keep it; optionally add an additive try_channel(u8)->Option backstop. The WS63 HIL suite (dma_mem_to_mem, hil.rs:250) can validate TransferCount, the cache self-service, the channel guard, and the WS63 clock path at register/poll level (it already clean/invalidates and polls channel_enabled). OPEN, needs on-board measurement: the BS2X CLK_AUTO_CTRL register offset/mask is unconfirmed (fbb_bs2x not in tree) — until a BS2X board measurement provides it, BS2X DMA clocking stays None/unverified, and the WS63 HIL rig cannot reach a BS2X part to close this. The peripheral-select enum swap is also not WS63-HIL-observable beyond compile because there is no peripheral-paced DMA jumper in the loopback suite.

designs.driver: i2c (WS63 i2c.rs custom v150 + BS2X i2c_v151.rs DesignWare)
designs.summary: The WS63 path is the dangerous one: `configure_i2c(idx, freq: u32)` writes `half = I2C_CLOCK_HZ/(4*freq)` as a full u32 into a register field that is only 16 bits wide on both the PAC (`SclHW = FieldWriter<...,16,u16>`, ws63-pac/src/lib.rs:8659/8700) and the vendor regs_def (`scl_h:16`), so freq <= 91 Hz silently truncates (class A) and freq >= 6_000_001 Hz produces half==0 / a dead bus (class B), while the freq==0 clamp to 1 Hz (class D) just steers a caller bug straight into the worst truncation case. The fix mirrors PWM exactly: replace the bare `freq: u32` with a construct-time-validated `I2cFreq(u16)` newtype whose `try_from_hz(pclk, hz) -> Option` returns `None` unless the resulting half-period is in 1..=0xFFFF AND hz is in the vendor band 1..=3_400_000 -- once an `I2cFreq` exists it is guaranteed programmable. The new constructors take `I2cFreq` (breaking). BS2X already enums Speed (safe by construction) so its only debt is the class-C wrong-rate magic SCL counts; I keep the enum and add an internal derivation from I2C_CLOCK_HZ plus a doc note. All embedded-hal `I2c`/`ErrorType`/async trait impls are untouched (operational surface stays u8 + &[u8] + Result). OPEN QUESTION needing on-board measurement: the true WS63 I2C source clock is unresolved (24 MHz driver const vs 40 MHz vendor porting default vs 80 MHz HAL doc); the newtype takes pclk as a parameter so the validation predicate is correct for whichever value silicon confirms, but the accept/reject boundary (91 Hz vs ~152 Hz vs ~305 Hz low edge; 6 MHz vs 10 vs 20 MHz dead edge) cannot be pinned until the clock is scoped. The HIL suite can validate the truncation register-level today (write I2cFreq, read back scl_h/scl_l), but spec-correct SCL timing needs a scope.

designs.surface: WS63 new_i2c0/new_i2c1 -> configure_i2c: replace `freq: u32` with a validated `I2cFreq` newtype (covers the class-A low-end truncation surface)
designs.validation_bounds: Accept iff hz != 0 AND hz <= 3_400_000 AND 1 <= pclk/(4*hz) <= 0xFFFF (65535). For pclk=24_000_000: low edge half>0xFFFF at hz<=91 (24e6/(4*65535)=91.55) -> reject 0..=91; high edge half==0 at hz>6_000_000 but the 3.4 MHz vendor cap rejects earlier, so the effective accept band is 92 Hz ..= 3_400_000 Hz. If silicon confirms 40 MHz: reject hz<=152; if 80 MHz: reject hz<=305. The half() result is always in 1..=65535, so the write is never truncated.
designs.embedded_hal_note: embedded_hal::i2c::{I2c, ErrorType, Error} and the embedded-hal-async I2c impls for I2c<'_,I2c0/I2c1> are untouched -- transaction()/write()/read()/write_read() keep their u8 + &[u8] + Result<(),I2cError> signatures. I2cFreq is an inherent-constructor (config-surface) type only, so it never appears in a trait signature.

designs.surface: WS63 configure_i2c high-end half==0 dead-bus (freq >= 6_000_001) -- subsumed by the I2cFreq upper-bound + vendor 3.4 MHz cap
designs.validation_bounds: Reject any hz that yields half==0, i.e. hz > pclk/4 (6_000_000 at 24 MHz, 10_000_000 at 40 MHz, 20_000_000 at 80 MHz). In practice the vendor cap hz > 3_400_000 rejects first, so the entire 3.4 MHz..u32::MAX dead band is unrepresentable. No I2cFreq can ever program a zero-length clock phase.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No trait surface involved -- identical to the row above; operational embedded-hal I2c impls unchanged.

designs.surface: WS63 configure_i2c freq==0 silent clamp to 1 Hz (`let freq = if freq == 0 {1} else {freq}`, i2c.rs:43)
designs.validation_bounds: hz == 0 -> None at construction (matches vendor i2c_init_check which returns ERRCODE_I2C_RATE_INVALID for baudrate==0, i2c.c:933/1284). A 0-Hz request can no longer be silently converted into a 36224-count garbage rate; the caller gets None and must choose a real frequency.
designs.embedded_hal_note: None -- pure config path. embedded-hal trait impls untouched.

designs.surface: WS63 new_i2c0/new_i2c1 infallible `-> Self` contract (no speed-mode/range validation on a bare u32)
designs.validation_bounds: Identical band to I2cFreq::try_from_hz: only 92 Hz..=3_400_000 Hz (at 24 MHz) is constructible. The previously-reachable unrunnable bands (0..=91 truncate, 3.4M..u32::MAX dead) cannot produce an I2cFreq, so they cannot reach new_i2c*. Mirrors the PWM newtype pattern (construct-time-validated, returns Self once the typed value exists).
designs.embedded_hal_note: new_i2c* are inherent constructors, not embedded-hal; free to retype. All I2c/ErrorType/async trait impls stay byte-for-byte identical.

designs.surface: WS63 configure_i2c I2C_CLOCK_HZ source-clock constant (24 vs 40 vs 80 MHz, unverified)
designs.validation_bounds: Not a type predicate -- a board precondition. If the real clock is 40 MHz a requested 100 kHz currently runs at ~167 kHz (1.67x fast); at 80 MHz ~333 kHz (3.33x). No accept/reject bound can be fixed until a scope measures SCL: confirm the actual I2C source clock, then set I2C_CLOCK_HZ and re-derive the documented low/dead edges (91/6M at 24 MHz, 152/10M at 40 MHz, 305/20M at 80 MHz).
designs.embedded_hal_note: None -- a const + doc only. No trait impls touched.

designs.surface: WS63 configure_i2c SCL formula fidelity (omits vendor `-1` and the scl_h/(scl_h+scl_l) weighting)
