designs.validation_bounds: With the `-1`: accept iff 1 <= (pclk/(4*hz)) - 1 <= 0xFFFF, i.e. pclk/(4*hz) >= 2, tightening the high-frequency edge by one count. The skew this removes is largest exactly there (e.g. half=1 was +100% fast). This is a class-D low-severity refinement; gate it behind a scope check confirming the resulting SCL period equals the request before committing the -1, since the omitted-(-1) behaviour is currently what the HIL loopback passes with.
designs.embedded_hal_note: None -- internal divider math. No trait surface.

designs.surface: BS2X configure: hardcoded magic SCL counts (1,160,190)/(2,40,50) not derived from I2C_CLOCK_HZ (class-C wrong-rate)
designs.validation_bounds: Each cnt must fit the 16-bit cnt field (bs2x-pac:6296) -- holds for any clk up to ~6.5 GHz at 100 kHz, so no overflow. The derived values reproduce the current (160,190)/(40,50) at clk=32 MHz exactly, so this is rate-neutral until the true BS2X I2C clock is confirmed; flagged class-C unverified because the vendor v151 source is absent from this checkout and the absolute timing cannot be cross-checked.
designs.embedded_hal_note: BS2X i2c_v151 exposes inherent write/read/write_read/probe (no embedded-hal I2c impl in this module today), so nothing trait-level changes; scl_params stays a private const fn.

designs.surface: BS2X Speed enum exposes only Standard/Fast (no High-Speed, no arbitrary rate)
designs.validation_bounds: No predicate change. Both current variants map to valid IC_CON.speed values (1=SS, 2=FS) and in-range cnt fields; nothing reachable is unrunnable. HS (speed=3) intentionally remains unrepresentable rather than half-implemented.
designs.embedded_hal_note: None -- no trait impls involved; enum is a config-surface input only.

designs.summary: The clock driver is probe/reference code, not a numeric Config-struct surface, so there are no class-A register-field-overflow defects to fix with bound-checked newtypes; the tightening is mostly fixing a polarity bug, replacing the assumed()/init_clocks "boot ROM did it" preconditions with a fallible read+verify Result API, and encoding the XIP/SRAM and PLL-up preconditions into type-state and ownership so an unrunnable init is unrepresentable. The single highest-value change is fixing TcxoFreq::detect's inverted strap polarity (bit0==1 -> MHz24) AND making it a fallible reader that returns a strap-derived value rather than guessing, then deleting the dishonest SystemClocks::assumed() const fabricator in favor of probe_clocks() as the only constructor. init_clocks is split into a typed sequencer that (a) takes ownership of CldoCrg by value, (b) is gated behind an ExecFromSram type-state token the caller can only mint after relocating out of XIP, (c) verifies PLL lock BEFORE switching any source mux (returning Err and switching nothing if unlocked), and (d) applies the per-TCXO TRNG/TSENSOR/I2C/Timer/WDT dividers the vendor switch_clock does. OPEN ON-BOARD QUESTION (HIL register-poll only confirms part of it): the polarity fix is a source-contradiction provable statically, but it still wants a known-24MHz board to confirm corrected detect() now reads MHz24 on bit0==1; and whether init_clocks actually hangs under XIP (the ExecFromSram guard's necessity) cannot be HIL-validated at register level — it needs a scope/board-population observation of a fetch-clock glitch, so that guard is justified by vendor evidence + prose, not the HIL suite.

designs.surface: TcxoFreq::detect (clock_init.rs:103-107) — inverted HW_CTL bit0 strap polarity (defect D, source contradiction)
designs.validation_bounds: Accept predicate: (hw_ctl & 0x1) == 1 => MHz24 (24_000_000); (hw_ctl & 0x1) == 0 => MHz40 (40_000_000). This is the EXACT inverse of the current clock_init.rs:106 predicate. Bound values from vendor soc_porting.h:22-23 (CLK24M_TCXO=1, CLK40M_TCXO=0) and soc_porting.c:23, and from the HAL's own soc/ws63.rs:97 which already uses (hw_ctl & 0x1)==1 -> 24M. Silicon datum: a 40 MHz board reads HW_CTL=0x2 (bit0 clear), so bit0==0 must map to MHz40.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal surface here at all — TcxoFreq is an inherent enum; no trait impls touched.

designs.surface: init_clocks (clock_init.rs:197-253) — switch-then-verify ordering (defect B); switches flash/UART/SPI muxes to PLL then polls lock
designs.validation_bounds: Accept (proceed to switch sources) iff REG_EXCEP_RO_RG @0x4000_319C bit12 == 1 within retry_count=30 polls at retry_delay_us=1000. Reject (Err(PllNotLocked), switch nothing) otherwise. Bound values: bit12 lock from clock_init.rs:129; the success SystemClocks always has pll_locked=true because the unlocked case now returns Err instead of a 'structurally valid but damaging' tcxo-fallback struct. Ordering rule from vendor switch_clock (clock_init.c:99): reference/PLL up before any source switch.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal surface; init_clocks is a free function. Drivers built afterward keep their unchanged embedded-hal impls (SpiBus, Read/Write, etc.).

designs.surface: init_clocks (clock_init.rs:197-214) — XIP-unsafe flash-clock manipulation precondition (defect C), unenforced
designs.validation_bounds: Not a numeric bound — a presence predicate: init_clocks is callable iff the caller can produce an ExecFromSram, which only `unsafe fn assume()` mints. Evidence the precondition is real: vendor switch_flash_clock_to_pll runs only inside flashboot from SRAM after sfc_flash_init (flashboot main.c:98); soc/ws63.rs:66 documents 'XIP-unsafe full clock_init'. The unused _sys_ctl0/_cldo_crg args (clock_init.rs:197) proved nothing today; the token makes the SRAM precondition load-bearing.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal interaction; ExecFromSram is a HAL-internal capability ZST.

designs.surface: init_clocks (clock_init.rs:197-253) — ignores &SysCtl0/&CldoCrg (_sys_ctl0/_cldo_crg), no exclusive CRG ownership; other live CldoCrg refs can race (defect C)
designs.validation_bounds: No numeric bound — an ownership predicate: at most one CldoCrg<'_> exists (peripheral singleton from peripherals.rs take()/steal()), so taking it by value makes a concurrent writer to CLDO_CRG_CLK_SEL/CKEN_CTL unrepresentable during init_clocks' non-atomic RMW. Evidence: clock_init.rs:197 args are unused (_sys_ctl0/_cldo_crg) and the body writes raw 0x4400_1134/0x4400_1104 pointers, so nothing today prevents a race.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal surface; consumes/returns the HAL CldoCrg singleton wrapper.

designs.surface: init_clocks PLL-up precondition (clock_init.rs:197-253, doc 166-169) — PLL assumed-up, never powered/configured (defect C)
designs.validation_bounds: Guard predicate (shared with the ordering surface): proceed iff REG_EXCEP_RO_RG @0x4000_319C bit12 == 1 within 30 polls; else Err(PllNotLocked) and no mux is moved. This converts the unenforced 'boot ROM already locked the PLL' assumption (clock_init.rs:166-169, :17-18) into a runtime-verified Result. We deliberately do NOT add a fabricated power-up: the vendor RF sequence (clock_init.c:59-138) is unverified on silicon for the HAL, so doc-and-guard is the honest choice for the analog-provisioning part.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal surface affected.

designs.surface: init_clocks (clock_init.rs:216-253) — missing per-TCXO TRNG/TSENSOR/I2C/Timer/WDT divider setup vendor switch_clock does (defect B)
designs.validation_bounds: Field-bounded RMW (the one place close to class-A): TRNG = DIV_CTL9 bits[8:6] (3-bit field, max 7) set to 2 (24M) / 4 (40M); TSENSOR = DIV_CTL7 bits[11:6] (6-bit field, max 0x3F) set to 0x18 (24M) / 0x28 (40M) — both in range, so write masked, never truncate. I2C/Timer/WDT counting clock = tcxo.hz() (24_000_000 / 40_000_000). Bound values from clock_init.c:119-138 (DIV_CTL9/DIV_CTL7 per-TCXO) and clock_init.c:142-166 (REQ_24M/REQ_40M). None of these registers are touched today (clock_init.rs:216-253).
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal surface in clock_init; the benefit is that downstream Trng/Tsensor/I2c/Timer/Watchdog drivers (whose embedded-hal impls are unchanged) now run at the correct rate without each re-deriving the divider.

designs.surface: Peripheral::cken_info (clock.rs:59-81) — None for 11 default-on peripherals, incl. Spi1 (defect C, reference-table precondition)
designs.validation_bounds: Same numeric invariant as today for the Ok arm: reg in {0,1} (CKEN_CTL0/CTL1), bit < 32 (1<<bit fits the word). The change is purely the absent-gate channel: Err(NoIndividualGate) for the 10 default-on blocks vs Err(GateUnverified) for Spi1 specifically. Provenance bounds from clock.rs:62-79 and module doc clock.rs:9-18: Spi0 Some((1,25)) confirmed (spi_porting.c:697); Spi1 unmapped (shares Spi0's domain, no attested independent bit).
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal surface — cken_info is an inherent reference method consumed by safety.rs; no trait impls.

designs.surface: SystemClocks::assumed (clock_init.rs:165-170) — const fabricator hard-codes tcxo_freq=MHz40, pll_locked=true (defect D, silent guess)
designs.validation_bounds: Removal predicate: there is no accept/reject bound to add — the bug is that assumed() accepts NO input yet asserts MHz40/true. Replacement with_tcxo(tcxo) makes tcxo a required TcxoFreq argument (only MHz24 or MHz40 representable) and keeps pll_locked=true ONLY as a documented const-context convenience, never as a runtime substitute for probe_clocks/init_clocks. Bound rationale: real boards are often 24 MHz (soc/ws63.rs:35,48 TCXO_HZ=24_000_000), so the MHz40 default at clock_init.rs:168 is the silent 40/24 timing error.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal surface; SystemClocks is an inherent struct.

designs.summary: The sfc driver has two highest-confidence silicon bugs (bus_dma_len off-by-one, and rd/wr_enable never set) plus a skewed/reserved SpiIfType encoding, all of which make the documented operation silently produce wrong/no result and are directly contradicted by the vendor regs_op. The fix is a SpiIfType enum re-encoded to the 5 real hardware values {0,1,2,5,6} (so reserved 3/4 are unrepresentable), a DmaLen newtype that stores n=len-1 and rejects 0/over-window, a BusConfig that cannot be built without rd/wr_enable and that RMW-preserves the write/read half, validated-newtype timing fields (Tshsl in 3..=15, Tcss/Tcsh in 0..=7), a CmdAddr newtype rejecting >=0x4000_0000, and a per-command IfType/dummy plumbed through send_command/command_with_data. No class-C clock-gate self-enable applies: SFC is the boot/XIP controller and is clocked by reset default (clock.rs returns cken_info=None for Sfc, correct). The class-C XIP-running and addr-mode write-ordering preconditions are genuinely not type-expressible (the type system cannot know whether the CPU is currently fetching from the flash window) and stay doc-and-guard. OPEN QUESTION needing on-board measurement: whether reserved iftype encodings 3/4 actually hang vs silently degrade to standard on silicon (only needed to confirm the enum's reject behaviour is conservative-correct), and whether the SFC bus window upper bound is 0x9FFFFF (vendor SFC_MCPU_END) vs the PAC mem_saddr 0xBFFFFF doc — these two windows disagree and the tighter 0x9FFFFF should be used until a board read confirms which the silicon enforces.

designs.surface: configure_bus: SpiIfType enum (read_if_type / write_if_type) discriminants cast into 3-bit rd_mem_if_type[0:2]/wr_mem_if_type[16:18]
designs.validation_bounds: Accept exactly {0,1,2,5,6}; reject {3,4} and all >6 by construction (no variant exists). Derived from vendor g_flash_common_read_cmds/g_flash_common_write_cmds 3rd field: 0x3B->1, 0xBB->2, 0x6B->5, 0xEB->6, quad write 0x32->5 (flash_config_info.c:77-93); PAC doc 000=Standard;001=Dual;010=Dual-IO;101=Quad-Input;110=Quad-IO (lib.rs:17186-17205).
designs.embedded_hal_note: SpiIfType is an sfc-inherent config type, not part of any embedded-hal trait; no embedded-hal::spi impl exists on SfcDriver, so nothing trait-facing changes.

designs.surface: configure_bus: rd_enable[bit31]/wr_enable[bit30] never set + blind full write of BUS_CONFIG1 instead of RMW; missing BUS_CONFIG2
designs.validation_bounds: rd_enable=bit31, wr_enable=bit30 always programmed to 1 (PAC lib.rs:17258-17265; vendor hal_sfc_v150_regs_op.c:72,85). dummy fields 0..=7 (3-bit), prefetch 0..=3 (2-bit) validated at construction (None otherwise). Two-step modify() = vendor RMW (regs_op.c:68-87) so neither half clobbers the other or the enable bits.
designs.embedded_hal_note: configure_bus is inherent, not embedded-hal; no SpiBus/Read/Write impl touches BUS_CONFIG1, so trait surface is unchanged.

designs.surface: configure_bus / bus reconfigure while executing from flash (XIP-running precondition)
designs.validation_bounds: No accept/reject predicate expressible: the precondition is 'CPU PC is not in 0x200000..0x9FFFFF right now', which is a runtime execution-context fact a value type cannot encode. Vendor serializes via hal_sfc_regs_wait_ready + g_dma_busy guard and runs reconfig from RAM (hal_sfc_v150.c:48-69,148-189).
designs.embedded_hal_note: Doc-only; no trait impl involved.

designs.surface: bus_dma_start: length -> bus_dma_len (off-by-one, no -1; length=0 wraps to 0x3FFF_FFFF)
designs.validation_bounds: Accept bytes in 1..=2^30 (1073741824); reject 0 and >2^30. Stored field = bytes-1 in 0..=0x3FFF_FFFF, the 30-bit width. Derived from PAC bus_dma_len 30-bit 'DMA length (n+1 bytes)' (lib.rs:17533-17545) and vendor 'dma_len = length - 1' (regs_op.h:114).
designs.embedded_hal_note: bus_dma_start is inherent; no embedded-hal DMA trait exists on SfcDriver, so unaffected.

designs.surface: bus_dma_start: mem_addr (and flash op range) unchecked against SFC bus window 0x200000..0x9FFFFF
designs.validation_bounds: Accept mem_addr in 0x200000..=0x9FFFFF AND mem_addr + (bytes-1) <= 0x9FFFFF; reject otherwise (SfcError::AddressOverstep). Derived from sfc_porting.c:15-16 SFC_MCPU_START 0x200000 / SFC_MCPU_END 0x9FFFFF and check_opt_param (sfc.c:39-58). NOTE: PAC mem_saddr doc says 0x00100000~0x00BFFFFF (lib.rs:17451) -- windows disagree; use the tighter vendor 0x9FFFFF pending an on-board read.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Inherent method; no embedded-hal trait touched.

designs.surface: bus_dma_start: bus_dma_ahb_ctrl never written (bursts off) + dma_sel_cs left at 0 (CS0) instead of vendor 1 (CS1)
designs.validation_bounds: No value predicate; the change is unconditional: bus_dma_ahb_ctrl=0x7 (incr4/8/16 bits 0/1/2, lib.rs:17574-17597; vendor regs_op.h:121-126) written before start, and dma_sel_cs=bit4=1 (CS1) per vendor regs_op.h:136. CS choice surfaced as an explicit ChipSelect param so board wiring is a typed choice, not a silent default.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Inherent; no trait impl.

designs.surface: send_command / command_with_data: address -> cmd_addr (full u32 into 30-bit field, top 2 bits truncated)
designs.validation_bounds: Accept addr in 0..=0x3FFF_FFFF; reject >=0x4000_0000. Derived from PAC cmd_addr FieldWriter<30,u32> 'Bits 0:29 - Operation address' (lib.rs:17827-17839). Class A truncation; benign for <=16MB flash but unrepresentable-bad-value is the correct contract.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Inherent command API; no embedded-hal trait. Folding address_enable into Option<CmdAddr> is an ergonomic + correctness improvement, still non-trait.

designs.surface: configure_timing: tcss / tcsh (3-bit, silent & 0x07 wrap)
designs.validation_bounds: Accept 0..=7; reject 8.. (was silently wrapped mod 8: 8->0, 9->1). Derived from PAC tcss/tcsh FieldWriter<3> 'Bits 8:10'/'Bits 12:14' (lib.rs:16904-16909); effective = field+1 (regs_def hal_sfc_v150_regs_def.h:76-80).
designs.embedded_hal_note: configure_timing is inherent; no embedded-hal trait touched.

designs.surface: configure_timing: tshsl (4-bit, silent mask-then-floor clamp to MIN_TSHSL-2)
designs.validation_bounds: Accept field in 3..=15; reject <3 and >15. Was: ((tshsl & 0x0F).max(3)) -- silently raised 1/2->3 and masked 20->4 (sfc.rs:154, MIN_TSHSL=5). Derived from PAC tshsl FieldWriter<4> (lib.rs:16900-16901), effective = field+2 (regs_def:73-74).
designs.embedded_hal_note: Inherent config; no embedded-hal trait.

designs.surface: send_command / command_with_data: mem_if_type hardcoded Standard (0<<17), dummy_byte_cnt never set
designs.validation_bounds: if_type accepts only {0,1,2,5,6} (via SpiIfType); dummy_bytes accepts 0..=7 (3-bit, lib.rs:17657-17676, regs_def:242-255), reject 8.. . Was: always 0<<17 (Standard) and no dummy (sfc.rs:308,363).
designs.embedded_hal_note: Inherent command path; no embedded-hal trait impl on SfcDriver, so unaffected.

designs.surface: configure_global: addr_mode (flash_addr_mode[bit2]) write-ordering precondition (no effect while CMD_CONFIG[start]=1)
designs.validation_bounds: Reject (SfcError::Busy) when CMD_CONFIG[start]=1; otherwise write. Derived from PAC doc 'flash_addr_mode: write has no effect when CMD_CONFIG[start]=1' (lib.rs:16774,16842). Runtime guard, not a value bound -- the precondition is dynamic controller state.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Inherent; no embedded-hal trait. Return type changes () -> Result, breaking for callers but trait-irrelevant.

designs.surface: Clock gating for Peripheral::Sfc (audit notes NO clock-gate defect)
designs.validation_bounds: N/A -- no class-C clock-gate defect exists for SFC. SFC is the peripheral the core boots from; its clock is up by reset default before any HAL code runs (clock.rs:78 cken_info=None is correct).
designs.embedded_hal_note: No trait or driver change.

designs.summary: The gpio driver's tightening centers on making "a pin value exists" imply "this pin is physically present, correctly muxed, on the right chip" — none of which the current bare-u8 + assert!/unreachable! surface guarantees. The highest-leverage change is a validated `GpioPin` newtype (Option ctor over the exact WS63 map: GPIO0 0..=7, GPIO1 8..=15, GPIO2 16..=18, i.e. block 2 only bits 0..=2) that replaces every bare `u8` taken by `AnyPin::steal`, the legacy free functions, and `IoConfigDriver`'s pin args; this kills both the silent no-op (pins 19..=23 → GPIO2 phantom pads) and the `unreachable!()` panic (pin ≥ 24 → block ≥ 3) in one type. `set_uart_mux` is a clean class-A overflow (0x07 mask into a 2-bit field) fixed by a `UartSel` enum/`u8`-newtype bounded 0..=3. `configure_*_pad`'s full `.write()` zeroing of IE/mode/ST bits is a class-D silent clamp fixed by switching to RMW (`.modify`) — no type change, but it must be paired with the apply_pull lesson. Two class-C preconditions cannot be expressed by value-types and need type-state / chip-gating instead: (1) the pin-mux-must-be-GPIO precondition, addressed by routing `init_*` through a `GpioFunction` capability obtained from `IoConfigDriver::into_gpio(pin)` so a GPIO driver is unconstructable without having set the mux; and (2) the chip-gating — `gpio` and `ulp_gpio` must become `#[cfg(feature = "chip-ws63")]` (or have apply_pull/ULP-clock paths gated) because the inline 0x4400_D000 IO_CONFIG write and the 32K ULP clock provisioning are WS63-only and silently wrong on BS2X. OPEN QUESTIONS needing on-board measurement: (a) does the WS63 reset-default mux already select GPIO (sel=0) for the blinky pads — if yes the mux-capability type is a correctness-hardening, not a bug-fix, and the silicon-validated blinky is setting mux out-of-band; (b) whether GPIO2 pads 19..=23 truly no-op or alias something on silicon; (c) whether ROM leaves the ULP 32K clock running (gpio_ulp_int_en is a WS63 stub) or enable_interrupt silently never fires — determines if enable_interrupt must self-enable a clock gate (class C) or just gain a doc-and-guard.

designs.surface: AnyPin::steal(pin: u8) + the legacy gpio::create_input_pin/create_output_pin(pin: u8) free functions (gpio.rs:101/549/557)
designs.validation_bounds: ACCEPT pin in 0..=18 (block in {0,1,2}; for block==2, bit in 0..=2). REJECT pin in 19..=23 (GpioId::from_pin returns None — these are GPIO2 bits 3..=7 with no physical pad per vendor GPIO_CHANNEL_2 pin_num=3) and pin >= 24 (block >= 3, regs() has no Gpio3). Bound 18 is the inclusive max; 16..=18 → block 2 bits 0..=2.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Untouched: digital::InputPin/OutputPin/StatefulOutputPin for Input/Output/Flex/GpioPin all operate on an already-constructed pin and keep their Infallible Result signatures. Only the construction surface (steal + the two free fns) changes from u8 to GpioId.

designs.surface: AnyPin::init_input / init_output / init_flex — the unenforced precondition that the pad mux must select the GPIO function before GPIO data/oen writes take effect (gpio.rs:126/133/145)
designs.validation_bounds: ACCEPT: a pad whose mux sel == 0 (GPIO function) before any GPIO data/oen write. REJECT (make unconstructable): calling init_* on a pad still on an alt-function (sel in 1..=4 per gpio_00_sel doc: 1:PWM0, 2:DIAG, 3:SPI1_CSN, 4:JTAG_TDI). The type forces sel=0 via into_gpio before a driver exists. Pads 15..=18 (UART-shared, no gpio_NN_sel) take the no-op into_gpio path and still yield a capability.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Untouched: all digital::* trait impls. This only adds a required argument to the inherent init_* constructors; the resulting Input/Output/Flex are identical and keep their embedded-hal impls.

designs.surface: gpio module chip-gating + apply_pull's inline 0x4400_D000 IO_CONFIG write reachable on BS2X (gpio.rs:404/423, lib.rs:42)
designs.validation_bounds: ACCEPT raw write to IO_CONFIG_BASE 0x4400_D000 + 0x800 + pin*4 ONLY under feature=chip-ws63 and pin in 0..=14 (the pads that have a pad_gpio_NN_ctrl register). REJECT (compile-out) on chip-bs21: bs2x-pac has zero gpio_NN_sel/pad_gpio_NN_ctrl regs and 0x4400_D000 is not the BS2X pin-control block, so the write must not be emitted. Pins 15..=18 remain a runtime no-op (already handled by the pin>14 early return).
designs.embedded_hal_note: Untouched: the digital::* impls are chip-independent; gating the module just stops the WS63 variant from compiling on BS2X. embedded-hal contract is preserved per-chip.

designs.surface: IoConfigDriver::set_uart_mux(function: u8) — masks function & 0x07 into a 2-bit hardware field (io_config.rs:193)
designs.validation_bounds: ACCEPT sel in 0..=3 (uart0_txd_sel is FieldWriter<REG, 2>; HAL_PIN_UART_SEL_BITS_NUM=2). REJECT 4..=7: the old `function & 0x07` let bit 2 reach the register, writing into a reserved/adjacent bit. The enum can only encode 0/1/2 (documented valid values), so bit-2 overflow is unrepresentable. Contrast set_gpio_mux whose 0x07 mask is CORRECT (gpio_NN_sel is FieldWriter<REG, 3>, HAL_PIN_GPIO_SEL_BITS_NUM=3) and stays as-is.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Untouched: set_uart_mux is an inherent IoConfig method, not an embedded-hal trait method — free to take an enum. No digital/SpiBus/etc. signature changes.

designs.surface: IoConfigDriver::configure_gpio_pad / configure_uart_pad / configure_sfc_pad — full .write() zeroes every pad-ctrl bit not in build_pad_ctrl, clearing IE/mode/ST for that pad (io_config.rs:218/280/308)
designs.validation_bounds: ACCEPT: configuring DS[6:4]/PE,PS[10:9]/ST[3]/IE[11] for a pad without disturbing mode bits[2:0], bits 7-8, or bits >=12. The vendor never full-writes (reg32_setbits, chip_io.h:265, is RMW of only [pos, pos+bits-1]). REJECT the old behavior: a full .write() with input_enable=false forced IE(bit11)=0, silently disabling the input buffer so subsequent reads return 0 regardless of pin. After the fix, an 'output pad' config leaves IE untouched unless input_enable is explicitly toggled.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Untouched: configure_*_pad are inherent methods, no embedded-hal involvement. Pure body change from .write to RMW; signatures unchanged.

designs.surface: ulp_gpio module gating + UlpGpioPin::enable_interrupt — ULP interrupt needs the 32K clock provisioned (driver never enables it) and the module is ungated (ulp_gpio.rs:80, lib.rs:156)
designs.validation_bounds: ACCEPT: an armed ULP interrupt fires only when the ULP GPIO clk == 32K (vendor gpio_ulp_int_en doc). REJECT silent-no-fire: enable_interrupt currently sets only gpio_int_en and never provisions 32K — if ROM does not leave the clock running, the IRQ never fires. The exact gate (which CKEN/CLK_SEL register selects 32K for UlpGpio) needs on-board confirmation; this is a class-C clock precondition that types cannot express, so it stays a guarded/documented self-enable.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Untouched: digital::InputPin/OutputPin/StatefulOutputPin for UlpGpioPin<Input/Output> keep Infallible Result signatures. enable_interrupt is an inherent method outside embedded-hal.

designs.surface: ulp_gpio::create_input_pin / create_output_pin(pin: u8) — computes bit = pin - 107 (u8 underflow/wrap) before assert!(bit < 8); panics on bad input instead of returning Option (ulp_gpio.rs:142/150)
designs.validation_bounds: ACCEPT pin in 107..=114 (8 ULP pads → bit 0..=7). REJECT pin < 107 (would underflow `pin - 107` in release builds — e.g. 99 → 248) and pin > 114 (bit >= 8). The new ctor compares before subtracting, so no wrap occurs and there is no panic — None is returned instead of the old assert!(bit < 8) runtime panic. Matches the PWM newtype Option pattern.
designs.embedded_hal_note: Untouched: digital::* impls for UlpGpioPin operate post-construction. Only the two free constructor fns change from u8 to UlpPinId.

designs.surface: Pull enum cannot express vendor value 2 = PIN_PULL_TYPE_STRONG_UP (missing-feature noted in audit; gpio.rs Pull / io_config.rs PullResistor)
designs.validation_bounds: ACCEPT the full vendor 2-bit pull space {PS,PE} in 0..=3: None=0, Down=1, StrongUp=2, Up=3 (vendor pin_pull_t; WS63 pull field HAL_PIN_PULL_START_BIT=9 BITS_NUM=2). The current enum can only reach 0/1/3, leaving value 2 (strong pull-up) unrepresentable. Adding the StrongUp variant fills the field exactly; no value is silently truncated. (The existing None/Down/Up encodings are already correct — verified-safe — so this is additive.)
designs.embedded_hal_note: Untouched: no embedded-hal trait carries pull; it is a config-struct field (InputConfig.pull) consumed by inherent init_input. Adding a variant only affects construction.

designs.driver: misc-sweep (delay.rs, efuse.rs, trng.rs, system.rs, usb.rs, pke.rs, spacc.rs, km.rs)
designs.summary: Eight surfaces need tightening; the unifying defect is "structurally valid but unrunnable on silicon" config that compiles. The two highest-leverage fixes are (1) make Delay carry the live clock instead of the const SYSTEM_CLOCK_HZ (the same Delay times an IRREVERSIBLE eFuse program pulse, so a wrong clock burns the OTP wrong), and (2) replace the free-u8 calibration setters (EfuseClkPeriod, FroDivCnt) with TcxoFreq-derived / range-checked newtypes — exactly the PWM precedent, reusing the EXISTING clock_init::TcxoFreq enum and SystemClocks struct rather than inventing new ones. system.rs must be #[cfg(chip-...)]-gated (its WS63 absolute MMIO is silently wrong on BS2X). TRNG gets a vendor-equivalent value/duplicate quality gate plus an enum for the sample-clock source. usb.rs/pke.rs/spacc.rs/km.rs are no-change (no writable-but-unrunnable surface today; the pke/spacc empty clock-enable is a latent class-C only once registers are actually written). OPEN MEASUREMENTS the types cannot settle and that gate the exact bounds: (a) the 3-cycles/loop Delay calibration and PLL-locked base must be scoped on 240 MHz WS63, 64 MHz BS21, and 40 MHz-crystal boards; (b) whether efuse access truly fails at clk_period=0 vs merely degrades; (c) the BS2X chip-reset / reset-record register addresses; (d) the minimum usable fro_div_cnt and whether an external TRNG sample clock is ever physically routed. The HIL suite (tests/hil.rs + hil/hil-smoke.sh) can validate every register-level fix (clk_period readback, fro_div behavior, reset-reason record, address-span rejection) but cannot by itself prove Delay wall-clock accuracy without a scope/GPIO-toggle measurement, and cannot prove the eFuse burn (irreversible — must stay experimental).

designs.surface: Delay::new (delay.rs:14) — const ZST with no clock argument; busy-loop derived from const SYSTEM_CLOCK_HZ assuming PLL locked
designs.validation_bounds: No reject predicate (any Hz is a legal clock); the tightening is that the clock is an explicit construction input, not an implicit const. from_clocks selects c.cpu_clk only when pll_locked, else c.tcxo_freq.hz() (24e6/40e6 on WS63, 32e6 on BS21) — eliminating the silent 240/24=10x or 240/40=6x error. cycles = hz as u64 * us / 1_000_000 (unchanged math, now over the real hz).
designs.embedded_hal_note: embedded_hal::delay::DelayNs impl is UNTOUCHED — delay_ns/delay_us/delay_ms keep their (&mut self, u32) -> () signatures; only the constructor and the internal hz source change.

designs.surface: Delay::delay_micros (delay.rs:19-27) — cycles_per_loop=3 hardcoded, body=spin_loop() with no guaranteed cycle cost
designs.validation_bounds: No accept/reject predicate — this is a calibration constant. The bound (CYCLES_PER_LOOP) MUST be derived by toggling a GPIO around a known delay and measuring on a scope, separately for WS63 (240 MHz) and BS21 (64 MHz). Until measured, keep =3 and document it as provisional; do not encode a false precision in a type.
designs.embedded_hal_note: DelayNs impl untouched; this only renames a local let into an associated const so it is visible and per-chip overridable.

designs.surface: EfuseDriver::set_clock_period (efuse.rs:85-89) — period: u8 accepts any value incl. reset 0; must match live TCXO (0x29@24MHz / 0x19@40MHz)
designs.validation_bounds: Accept exactly {0x29 for MHz24, 0x19 for MHz40} via for_tcxo; from_raw rejects 0 and accepts 1..=255. (Whether values other than 0x29/0x19 are merely degraded vs hard-fail is the open on-board measurement — if non-matching values prove harmful, drop from_raw and expose only for_tcxo/detected.) Bounds come from efuse_porting.c:115-124 / pm_porting.c:272-279.
designs.embedded_hal_note: eFuse exposes no embedded-hal trait (OTP is not a HAL trait domain); no trait signature is affected.

designs.surface: EfuseDriver::read_buffer (efuse.rs:119-124) — address = start_byte + i as u16 wraps/panics; only per-byte <256 guard, no up-front span check
designs.validation_bounds: Accept iff start_byte.checked_add(len).is_some() && start+len <= EFUSE_MAX_BYTES (256); reject otherwise with OutOfRange — BEFORE any read. This makes read_buffer(65530, [_;12]) return OutOfRange instead of wrapping to addresses 0..5. EFUSE_MAX_BYTES=256 (efuse_porting.h:25-28).
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait involved; read_buffer is an inherent method already returning Result, so the fix is additive within the existing signature.

designs.surface: EfuseDriver::write_byte (efuse.rs:133-149) — AVDD settle via CPU busy-loop Delay::new(); wrong clock under/over-burns an IRREVERSIBLE fuse; no verify
designs.validation_bounds: No numeric-field bound; the precondition is 'the supplied Delay reflects the live clock' (enforced by the Delay-from_clocks fix) + 'clk_period was set for the live TCXO' (enforced by EfuseClkPeriod). The vendor uses a HW TCXO delay (uapi_tcxo_delay_us, HAL_EFUSE_DELAY_US=100); a CPU busy-loop is acceptable ONLY when the Delay carries the true Hz. Ideal long-term: route to a hardware TCXO timebase.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait; passing &Delay keeps the operation inherent and explicit about its timing dependency.

designs.surface: TrngDriver::set_divider (trng.rs:119-123) — div: u8 accepts 0/1 (FRO sampled too fast => no entropy => permanent Timeout); working value 0x1b
designs.validation_bounds: Accept c >= MIN_FRO_DIV, reject below it. MIN_FRO_DIV is the ONE open bound the type cannot settle from registers alone — the only on-silicon-confirmed working value is 0x1b; a conservative floor is to expose only FroDivCnt::VENDOR (0x1b) plus from_count gated at the measured minimum. Until the minimum usable divider is measured, default MIN_FRO_DIV=0x1b (i.e. only the vendor value is constructible). Source: hal_trng_reg.h:25, hal_trng.c:57-60.
designs.embedded_hal_note: TRNG exposes no embedded-hal trait (rand traits are not embedded-hal 1.0); no trait signature affected.

designs.surface: TrngDriver::set_sample_clock (trng.rs:109-113) — external: bool; selecting external with no board-routed clock => FRO never sampled => permanent Timeout
designs.validation_bounds: No numeric bound. The enum replaces a bare bool so the call site reads FroSampleClock::External (self-documenting the precondition) instead of an opaque `true`. Whether External is ever physically wired is a board fact a type cannot assert — doc it as 'requires a provisioned external sample clock; otherwise read_blocking() Times out forever'. fro_sample_clk_sel bit0: 0=inner/1=external (ws63-pac lib.rs:29201).
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait; inherent setter only — enum is a drop-in for the bool argument.

designs.surface: TrngDriver::read / read_blocking / fill_bytes / fill_words (trng.rs:54-104) — raw FIFO passthrough; no vendor quality gate (rejects 0x0/0xFFFFFFFF/duplicate)
designs.validation_bounds: Reject (re-poll, never return as success) iff word == 0x00000000 || word == 0xFFFFFFFF || word == previous_word; accept any other value. Exactly the vendor predicate (hal_trng.c:111-113). On persistent degeneracy the existing 1,000,000-iteration budget still yields Timeout, so the signature/return type is unchanged — the gate just stops surfacing non-random words as success.
designs.embedded_hal_note: TRNG has no embedded-hal trait; read/read_blocking/fill_* are inherent and already return Result — the gate is purely additive, signatures unchanged. (read() one-shot may need a NoData-vs-stuck distinction or a new StuckEntropy variant.)

designs.surface: System::software_reset / software_reset_cpu / reset_reason (system.rs:42-97) — hardcoded WS63 absolute MMIO (0x4000_2110/_00A0/_00A4), NO chip gating; wrong/harmful on BS2X
designs.validation_bounds: No value bound — the predicate is chip identity: the WS63 absolute addresses are accept-only-on-chip-ws63. The BS2X addresses are the open measurement (item (c)); until confirmed, a compile_error! is preferable to writing a foreign MMIO that may have side effects or hang the reset spin loop. Source: reboot_porting.c (WS63-only); no BS2X equivalent found at these addresses.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait (reset is not a HAL trait domain); only the cfg gating and module-local addresses change, the public method signatures (reset_reason -> ResetReason, software_reset -> !) are unchanged.

designs.surface: usb.rs Usb::new / device_enumerate (no caller-writable config; devspd=0/devaddr=0 hardcoded valid; speed_from_enumspd total)
designs.validation_bounds: No config surface to bound. speed_from_enumspd is total over u8 (wildcard arm => Speed::Full, proven by enumspd_out_of_range_is_full + the proptest). The PHY/clock precondition is a board/boot fact a type here cannot express; worth an on-board enumeration check but out of scope for a config-surface defect.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait impls on Usb; nothing to preserve.

designs.surface: pke.rs / spacc.rs enable/disable/is_busy (stubs: empty/return-false no-ops, no register writes, no config fields)
designs.validation_bounds: No surface to bound now. Future bound: enable() must assert the security clock gate before any data-register write; is_busy() must read a real status bit, not return a constant false (currently it lies). These become class-C/B the moment registers are touched.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait impls; the existing CipherAlg/CipherMode/HashAlg enums are already the right typed shape for the eventual config surface.

designs.surface: km.rs is_keyslot_locked / lock_keyslot (slot: u8 guarded by assert!(slot < KEYSLOT_COUNT=8); 10-bit slot fields)
designs.validation_bounds: slot < 8 (KEYSLOT_COUNT), already enforced by assert!. 10-bit register field >> 3-bit needed range, so no truncation possible. Optional nicety: Keyslot::from_index(u8)->Option to turn the panic into a None, but not required.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait impls; methods stay inherent.

designs.summary: All three drivers are BS2X-only, inherent-method drivers with ZERO embedded-hal trait impls (verified: no `embedded_hal`/`embedded_io` reference in keyscan.rs/pdm.rs/qdec.rs), so every config tightening is free to type and provably cannot break any trait surface. The ONE true register-field overflow (defect A) is Keyscan::new(rows, cols): `rows.saturating_sub(1)` -> 5-bit row_pin_en (PAC RowPinEnW=FieldWriter<5>, lib.rs:24244) and `cols.saturating_sub(1)` -> 3-bit clo_pin_en (CloPinEnW=FieldWriter<3>, lib.rs:24240), both masked silently by FieldWriter::bits(); fix with two validated newtypes RowCount(1..=18) and ColCount(1..=8) whose `from_count` returns None, so the silent wrap becomes an un-constructable value — directly mirroring PwmPeriod/Duty. The dominant theme is the broken "construct -> clocked" contract that PWM honors: ALL THREE fail to self-enable their clock tree (defect C) and ALL THREE skip the vendor scan/sample/filter config (defect B). The faithful fix folds the vendor port functions into new(): keyscan pinmux+scan-timing, pdm_port_clock_enable (CRG glb gate 0x52000548 bit10 + clk-src mux 0x5200004c bit1 + 7 datapath sub-clocks), qdec_clk_sel_set (M_CTL 0x520004A0 clk-enable + sampleper) + qdec pinmux — implemented as private `enable_*_clock()`/`configure_*()` helpers exactly like pwm.rs `enable_pwm_clock()`. PDM/QDEC take no caller numerics today (gains/modes/thresholds hardcoded and in-range), so they need no newtype — only the clock/config self-enable. OPEN HARDWARE QUESTIONS for the WS63 HIL suite (it is register/poll-level, and these are BS2X parts so full HIL is partial at best): (1) confirm the silicon row maximum is truly 18 and column max truly 8 before freezing the newtype bounds — the PAC width (5-bit/3-bit) exceeds the vendor "valid 0-17 / 0-7", and per the PWM pwm_freq_h lesson the type must encode MEASURED reality not the regs_def; (2) confirm CRG bits (0x52000548 bit10, 0x5200004c bit1) and M_CTL 0x520004A0 clk-enable actually latch and yield a non-empty UP-FIFO / moving qdec_acc — needs a BS2X board + DMIC/encoder, which the current WS63 HIL rig cannot fully cover.

designs.surface: Keyscan::new(rows, cols) — cols argument -> clo_pin_en (3-bit field)
designs.validation_bounds: ACCEPT cols in 1..=8 (vendor KEYSCAN_MAX_COLUMN=8 / regs_def clo_pin_en valid 0-7); REJECT (None) for 0 and for >=9. field() = count-1 is always in 0..=7 so the 3-bit FieldWriter never truncates. Predicate: `n >= 1 && n <= 8`.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait is touched: keyscan.rs implements none (verified). KeyEvent / read_key()/read_error path are inherent and unchanged; only the inherent new() signature gains a typed parameter.

designs.surface: Keyscan::new(rows, cols) — rows argument -> row_pin_en (5-bit field)
designs.validation_bounds: ACCEPT rows in 1..=18 (vendor regs_def row_pin_en valid 0-17, so count-1 in 0..=17); REJECT (None) for 0 and >=19. field()=count-1 is 0..=17, well inside the 5-bit FieldWriter, so no wrap. Predicate: `n >= 1 && n <= 18`. NOTE: the 18 upper bound is from the vendor regs_def, NOT yet silicon-measured — per the PwmPeriod pwm_freq_h lesson, confirm 18 is the true physical row max on a BS2X board before freezing (the type must encode measured reality).
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait touched (keyscan.rs has none). Only the inherent new() parameter type changes; read_key() and the KeyEvent decode are unchanged.

designs.surface: Keyscan::new — GPIO pin mux (HAL_PIO_KEY_SCAN) + row pull-down/col pull-up + KEYSCAN_PIN_SEL_ROW/COL routing (defect C: dead output path)
designs.validation_bounds: Not a numeric bound. Driver-level self-enable (class C): fold keyscan_porting_pin_set into new() so 'construct -> scannable output path'. Residual genuinely-untypable precondition (physical matrix wiring) -> doc-and-guard. ACCEPT: any RowCount/ColCount; the helper muxes exactly that many lines.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait touched. Pure additive register writes inside the inherent new().

designs.surface: Keyscan::new — scan timing: pulse_time / scan_mode / wait_time / idle_time / defence / io_de (defect B: under-configured, no working scan)
designs.validation_bounds: Not a numeric caller surface. Defect B self-config: write the 6 vendor-default scan-timing fields in new() so the matrix is actually driven/debounced. All constants are fixed vendor defaults (pulse=10us, wait=30+5us, idle=3ms, 3-sample defence, io_de=true). If a future API exposes these as caller knobs they should each become a validated newtype (e.g. PulseTime micros bounded to the field width); for 0.5.0 they are hardcoded.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait touched. Additive inherent register writes only.

designs.surface: Pdm::new — PDM clock-source mux (0x5200004c bit1, XO sel) + CRG global gate (0x52000548 bit10, glb_clken_pdm) (defect C: whole block unclocked)
designs.validation_bounds: Not numeric. Driver-level self-enable (class C, the headline defect): fold pdm_port_clock_enable into new() -> 'construct -> clocked'. Predicate is bit-level: clear 0x5200004c bit1 (XO), set 0x52000548 bit10 (glb gate). HARDWARE-CONFIRM these bits latch on BS2X silicon before trusting them (per the pwm_freq_h non-latch lesson). Residual: external DMIC pad/AFE wiring stays a documented board precondition.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait touched (pdm.rs implements none). capture()/version() inherent methods unchanged.

designs.surface: Pdm::new — per-stage datapath clocks: cic_clken_0 / srcdn_clken_0 / hpf_clken_0 / compd_clken_0 / ckdiv_6144k_clken / saradc_clken / clk_freq_sel (defect B: no decimation / no DMIC bit clock)
designs.validation_bounds: Not numeric. Defect B self-config: enable the 7 missing datapath sub-clocks + clk_freq_sel=0 so decimation runs and the DMIC actually gets a bit clock. All bits/fields exist+writable in PAC clk_rst_en. Fixed values (all set_bit, clk_freq_sel=0); no caller-tunable range, so no newtype needed.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait touched. Single existing inherent register-write widened.

designs.surface: Pdm::new — AFE/DMIC pad provisioning; cic_gain (8-bit, const 0x14) / srcdn_mode (2-bit, const 0) hardcoded, no caller config surface
designs.validation_bounds: No bound to enforce: no caller numeric. cic_gain const 0x14 <= 0xFF (8-bit field) and srcdn_mode const 0 <= 3 (2-bit field) are statically in range. Pure board/analog precondition -> documentation only (genuinely untypable). If later exposed: CicGain::from_raw accepting full 0..=255, SrcdnMode as a 4-variant enum.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait touched. No signature change at all.

designs.surface: Qdec::new — QDEC clock select+enable (M_CTL_QDEC_CLK_CTL 0x520004A0: set QDEC_CLK_EN_BIT, freq divider) + sample period (M_CTL_QDEC_SAMPLEPER) (defect C: decoder never samples, read_count() stuck at 0)
designs.validation_bounds: Not a caller numeric. Driver-level self-enable (class C): fold qdec_clk_sel_set into new() so 'construct -> sampling'. Bit-level predicate: set 0x520004A0 bit7 (QDEC_CLK_EN_BIT), write the 7-bit divider (qdec_clk_div num 0..=127) + sampleper. The divider/sampleper are fixed vendor defaults for 0.5.0; if later exposed as caller knobs, QdecDivider(u8) from_count bounded 1..=127 (7-bit field). CONFIRM the 0x520004A0 enable+divider latch on BS2X silicon (pwm_freq_h lesson).
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait touched (qdec.rs implements none). read_count()/read_error_count()/stop() inherent methods unchanged.

designs.surface: Qdec::new — A/B GPIO pin mux (HAL_PIO_QDEC_A/B) + input dir + pull-up (defect C: dead input, no gray-code transitions)
designs.validation_bounds: Not numeric. Driver-level self-enable (class C): fold qdec_port_pinmux_init into new() so 'construct -> live input path'. Residual untypable precondition (physical encoder wiring) -> doc-and-guard.
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait touched. Additive inherent register writes only.

designs.surface: Qdec::new — sample period / debounce (defen_en/defen_num) / report period / LED config (hal_qdec_v150_init) (defect B: unreliable decode, bounce double-counts)
designs.validation_bounds: Not a caller numeric. Defect B self-config: write sampleper + defen_en/defen_num + reportper + ledpol vendor defaults so the decoder completes samples and debounces bounce. Fixed vendor defaults for 0.5.0; if any becomes a caller knob later it should be a validated newtype bounded to its field width (e.g. DefenNum(u8) from_count, SamplePer(u16) from_count non-zero).
designs.embedded_hal_note: No embedded-hal trait touched. Additive inherent register writes only.

surfaces.issue: new() only stores the peripheral token; it does NOT set the enable bit or clear the counter. The vendor init sequence (hal_tcxo_init) unconditionally does set_enable() THEN set_clear(). A caller who writes the obvious `TcxoDriver::new(tcxo).read_counter()` (or read_counter32) gets a counter that is not running: read_counter() issues a refresh and reads count0..3, but with enable=0 the free-running counter never advances, so it returns a frozen / zero value that is structurally valid but meaningless. Unlike the PWM reference (which self-enables its full clock tree in configure so 'construct -> clocked'), tcxo requires the caller to remember to call enable() first, and that precondition is unenforced and undocumented at the type level. read_counter/read_counter32 do NOT call enable() either.
surfaces.evidence: Vendor hal_tcxo_init does set_enable()+set_clear(): hal_tcxo_v150.c:19-30 (calls hal_tcxo_status_set_enable() then hal_tcxo_status_set_clear()). Rust new() at tcxo.rs:34-36 does neither; read_counter() at tcxo.rs:95-102 only refreshes. enable bit width confirmed: tcxo_status.enable bit 2, hal_tcxo_v150_regs_def.h:53.

surfaces.issue: Vendor init also issues a clear() at init so the counter starts from a defined zero base. new() omits this, so the first read_counter() after construction returns whatever stale/undefined count was latched from a prior boot/ROM stage rather than a zero-based monotonic value. Callers using the counter for a t0 reference (delay/elapsed measurement, the documented use case) get an undefined origin. This is a missing init step, not a numeric field.
surfaces.evidence: hal_tcxo_v150.c:28 (hal_tcxo_status_set_clear() in init). clear bit = tcxo_status.clear bit 1, hal_tcxo_v150_regs_def.h:52. Rust new() tcxo.rs:34-36 performs no clear.

surfaces.field: count0..count3 read width (.bits() vs 16-bit count field)
surfaces.issue: The driver assembles the 64-bit value from `tcxo_countN().read().bits()` (the full 32-bit register), shifting c1<<16, c2<<32, c3<<48 (and c1<<16 for the 32-bit path). But each count register's defined data field is only 16 bits [15:0]; bits [31:16] are not part of the count. If silicon does not guarantee the upper 16 bits read as zero (reserved/undefined upper half), the stray upper bits of c0 bleed into the c1 lane, c1 into c2, etc., silently corrupting the assembled count. The vendor explicitly masks every lane to 16 bits via the `.b.data` bitfield (and the PAC exposes a 16-bit-masked accessor countN() that the driver bypasses). This is a register-field truncation/corruption hazard on the read path that needs on-board confirmation of upper-half behavior.
surfaces.evidence: PAC fields are 16-bit masked: Count0R..Count3R = FieldReader<u16> with `(self.bits & 0xffff)` — ws63-pac/src/lib.rs ~29406-29512 (Bits 0:15 each, & 0xffff). Vendor masks via tmp_count.b.data (16-bit bitfield): hal_tcxo_v150_regs_op.c:44-54 and tcxo_count_data union data:16 at hal_tcxo_v150_regs_def.h:35. Driver uses .bits() (full u32) not the masked .countN(): tcxo.rs:97-101 and tcxo.rs:109-111.

surfaces.issue: refresh() polls the valid bit only 100 spin-loop iterations before returning Err(()) (surfaced as read_counter -> None). The doc comment justifies this as '~1ms at 32kHz TCXO clock', but the TCXO actually runs at 24MHz (or 40MHz), not 32kHz, per the vendor porting header — so the timing rationale is wrong by ~750x and the real wall-clock window is far shorter than intended. The vendor waits up to TCXO_LOCK_GET_ATTE = 0xFFFFFFFF attempts for the latch handshake. Under a short/wrong-sized budget, a healthy refresh can spuriously report failure (None) even when the hardware would have latched, making a valid configuration silently unrunnable for the caller.
surfaces.evidence: Rust loop bound 100: tcxo.rs:76 (`for _ in 0..100`) with comment claiming 32kHz at tcxo.rs:75. Vendor poll bound TCXO_LOCK_GET_ATTE 0xFFFFFFFF: tcxo_porting.h:29; vendor poll loop hal_tcxo_v150.c:44-49. True TCXO frequency 24MHz: tcxo_porting.h:21 (HAL_TCXO_TICKS_PER_SECOND 24000000), and 24M vs 40M selectable per soc_porting.c:23-26.

surfaces.field: refresh request bit not self-cleared; valid flag not re-armed before poll
surfaces.issue: refresh() sets TCXO_REFRESH_BIT then immediately polls TCXO_VALID_BIT. It never clears the refresh request afterward, and it does not first clear/observe a transition of the valid flag. If the valid bit is sticky (stays 1 from a previous refresh), the very first poll iteration returns Ok immediately and the subsequent count read returns the PREVIOUS latch, not the value latched by the refresh just issued — a stale-read race. Without on-board confirmation of whether valid auto-clears on a new refresh request, a structurally valid read_counter() call can return a stale counter (off by one refresh interval). The vendor's set_refresh is a read-modify-write that also leaves refresh set; it relies on hardware self-clearing the request and re-arming valid, which is unverified here.
surfaces.evidence: Driver sets refresh and polls valid with no re-arm: tcxo.rs:70-83. valid bit = tcxo_status.valid bit 4 (def at hal_tcxo_v150_regs_def.h:55, driver TCXO_VALID_BIT 1<<4 tcxo.rs:25). Vendor set_refresh is RMW leaving the bit set: hal_tcxo_v150_regs_op.c... set_refresh in hal_tcxo_v150_regs_op.h:67-73; vendor re-reads valid each loop hal_tcxo_v150.c:44-49.

audits.notes:
Scope note: the tcxo driver has NO numeric Config struct and NO caller-supplied set_*/configure value path — new() takes only the Tcxo<'d> peripheral token, and the count registers are read-only outputs. So there is no classic caller-passes-an-overflowing-value (class A on a Config field) surface here; the count-register field-width issue (class A) is on the READ path (driver bypasses the PAC's 16-bit-masked accessor and uses raw .bits()), which I report because it silently corrupts the assembled value if the upper half is non-zero on silicon. The dominant defects are class-C unenforced preconditions: new() does not self-enable/clear the counter the way the PWM reference self-enables its clock tree, so 'construct -> running counter' does NOT hold.

Chip gating: both ws63 and bs2x have identical TCXO v150 register layout (status + count0..3, all count fields 16-bit). ws63 base 0x4400_04C0 (ws63-pac/src/lib.rs:29276, matches vendor TCXO_COUNT_BASE_ADDR 0x440004C0 platform_core_rom.h:25). bs2x base 0x5700_0200 (bs2x-pac/src/lib.rs:5265). No separate clock-gate (CKEN) register is referenced for TCXO in the vendor porting layer — unlike PWM/UART, the TCXO porting code only sets a base address and ticks-per-usec; the counter is gated solely by tcxo_status.enable. So the missing self-enable in new() (defect 1) is the actual 'clock'/run gate for this peripheral.

NEEDS ON-BOARD CONFIRMATION: (a) whether count register bits [31:16] read as zero on silicon (decides severity of the raw .bits() read, defect 3); (b) whether tcxo_status.valid auto-clears on a new refresh request or is sticky (decides the stale-read race, defect 5); (c) the real latch latency at 24/40MHz to size the poll budget (defect 4). tcxo_status is PAC Writable with Safety=Unsafe and resets to 0; the driver's RMW enable/disable/clear/refresh preserve other bits correctly (verified by the in-file proptests), so the bit-manipulation itself is sound — the gaps are the missing init steps and the read-path width/race, not the bit math.

surfaces.issue: Field is a u8 (0..=255) but the hardware field is 5-bit (0..=31). The driver silently truncates with `config.sample_count & 0x1F` (lsadc.rs:150). A caller passing sample_count=32 programs 0, sample_count=40 programs 8, etc. — the scan timing silently differs from what was requested, with no error. Should be a construct-time-validated newtype (max 31) like PwmPeriod, not a raw u8 masked at write time.
surfaces.evidence: PAC SampleCntW = FieldWriter<5> (ws63-pac/src/lib.rs:21283; reader masks `(self.bits>>8)&0x1f` :21306). Vendor regs_def adc_ctrl_data.sample_cnt:5 (hal_adc_v154_regs_def.h:158). Driver mask `& 0x1F` at lsadc.rs:150.

surfaces.issue: Field is a u8 (0..=255) but the hardware field is 7-bit (0..=127). The driver silently truncates with `config.cast_count & 0x7F` (lsadc.rs:152). cast_count=128 programs 0, 200 programs 72, etc. — silent wrap of the cast/settling count with no signal. Should be a validated newtype (max 127).
surfaces.evidence: PAC CastCntW = FieldWriter<7> (ws63-pac/src/lib.rs:21291; reader masks `(self.bits>>21)&0x7f` :21316). Vendor regs_def adc_ctrl_data.cast_cnt:7 (hal_adc_v154_regs_def.h:160). Driver mask `& 0x7F` at lsadc.rs:152.

surfaces.issue: Field is a u8 (0..=255) but rxintsize is 3-bit (0..=7). The driver silently truncates with `level & 0x07` (lsadc.rs:169). level=8 programs waterline 0, level=14 programs 6, etc. — the RX-FIFO interrupt threshold silently differs from the requested value, so the conversion-complete IRQ fires at the wrong fill level (or at 0). Should be a validated newtype (max 7).
surfaces.evidence: PAC RxintsizeW = FieldWriter<3> (ws63-pac/src/lib.rs:21372; reader masks `self.bits & 7` :21383). Vendor regs_def adc_fifo_data.rxintsize:3 (hal_adc_v154_regs_def.h:132). Driver mask `& 0x07` at lsadc.rs:169. SDK default waterline = 0x6 (hal_adc_v154_regs_op.h:294).

surfaces.field: LSADC bus clock + soft-reset (CLDO cldo_crg_rst_soft_cfg_1 @ 0x4400_1138, soft_rst_lsadc_n bit5 / soft_rst_lsadc_bus_n bit7)
surfaces.issue: Unlike PWM (which self-enables its clock tree in configure so 'construct -> clocked'), LsAdc never enables its bus clock / deasserts the CLDO LSADC reset. new() just stores the token; enable() only sets da_lsadc_rstn (the ANALOG reset inside the lsadc block), not the CLDO bus reset. With the CLDO LSADC/LSADC_bus soft-reset still asserted, every configure_scan/start_scan/read MMIO access hits a held-in-reset block: writes don't latch and no conversion ever runs. A structurally valid, fully-configured driver silently produces no output on silicon.
surfaces.evidence: Vendor power-up calls hal_adc_soft_rst(0) then hal_adc_soft_rst(1) which toggles cldo_crg_rst_soft_cfg_1.soft_rst_lsadc_n + soft_rst_lsadc_bus_n at CLDO base 0x44001138 (adc_porting.c:22; hal_adc_v154_regs_op.h:57-64; hal_adc_v154.c:184-185). adc_port_clock_enable()/adc_port_init_clock() are empty stubs (adc_porting.c:39-47) — the soft-reset IS the gate. The Rust driver has no access to the CLDO block and never performs this (lsadc.rs:112-129 only touches lsadc_ctrl_11).

surfaces.field: AFE analog power-up sequence (da_lsadc_rwreg_1..3 + da_lsadc_en staged ORs + tcxo delays + offset/cap/gain calibration)
surfaces.issue: configure_scan + start_scan compile and look complete, but no conversion is produced unless the full AFE bring-up has run first: simu_cfg1=0x155240/cfg2=0x5/cfg3=0x100 to da_lsadc_rwreg_1..3, staged da_lsadc_en ORs of 0x7000 -> (500us) 0xE7F -> (300us) 0x100 -> 0x80, then offset/cap/gain auto-calibration, then da_lsadc_rstn release. None of this is in the driver; the doc admits it (lsadc.rs:22-26). enable() releases only da_lsadc_rstn. On silicon the FIFO stays empty forever -> read_sample returns None indefinitely and read_async parks on IRQ72 that never fires.
surfaces.evidence: hal_adc_simulation_cfg sequence with DELAY_500US/DELAY_300US (hal_adc_v154.c:58-71), simu writes to da_lsadc_rwreg_1..3 and staged da_lsadc_en |= 0x7000/0xE7F/0x100/0x80 (hal_adc_v154_regs_op.h:66-124). adc_port_calibration + adc_process_before_use_adc run before any sample (hal_adc_v154.c:152-192). da_lsadc_rwreg_1..3 registers are not even in the Rust RegisterBlock.

surfaces.issue: Field width is fine (u16 into a 16-bit field, no truncation), but the method does `w.da_lsadc_en().bits(bits)` inside modify (lsadc.rs:135-137) i.e. an OVERWRITE of the whole 16-bit field, whereas the silicon power-up REQUIRES four successive read-modify-OR steps (|= 0x7000, |= 0xE7F, |= 0x100, |= 0x80) separated by 500us/300us tcxo delays. A single raw write cannot reproduce the timed staged ramp, and overwriting clears bits a prior step set. Exposing the raw field with overwrite semantics invites a structurally-valid call that leaves the AFE half-enabled / no working analog output. No precondition (clock/reset up, delays) is enforced.
surfaces.evidence: SDK builds the field with read-modify-OR across timed steps: simu_ldo_start |=0x7000, simu_cfg4 |=0xE7F (after 500us), simu_cfg5 |=0x100 (after 300us), simu_cfg6 |=0x80 (hal_adc_v154_regs_op.h:94-124), sequenced with uapi_tcxo_delay_us in hal_adc_v154.c:58-71. PAC DaLsadcEnW = FieldWriter<16,u16> (ws63-pac/src/lib.rs:21776).

surfaces.issue: Width is fine (u8 into 8-bit cic_osr, no truncation), but this whole register pair has no validated working range and no effect on the default output path. The vendor SDK NEVER writes cfg_cic_osr or cfg_cic_filter_en anywhere; the documented sample path uses the cali pipeline, not the CIC filter. oversampling_ratio=0 enables the filter with a zero OSR (no/undefined decimation -> no usable output), and the CIC output only reaches the FIFO if cfg_data_sel.data_sel=1 (post-processed) is also selected — an unenforced coupling. A structurally valid enable_cic_filter(0) compiles and produces no working output.
surfaces.evidence: grep of the entire vendor drivers/ tree finds zero writes to cic_osr / cfg_cic_filter_en / cic_filter (only the regs_def declaration hal_adc_v154_regs_def.h:85-86). PAC CicOsrW = FieldWriter<8> (ws63-pac/src/lib.rs:22036), CicFilterEnW = BitWriter (:21995). No min/typ OSR documented.

surfaces.issue: Width is fine (u16 into 16-bit, no truncation), but on silicon these raw correction registers are dead for the supported flow: the SDK applies offset/gain correction exclusively through the calibration state machine (cfg_adc_offset_cali* and cfg_gain_cali_en) and never writes cfg_offset/cfg_gain. Their value only matters if cfg_data_sel selects post-processed output AND the post-processing/cali pipeline is provisioned — neither enforced. A caller setting offset/gain via these methods sees no change to conversion results in the default (raw) path: structurally valid, no observable effect.
surfaces.evidence: SDK writes cfg_adc_offset_cali / cfg_adc_offset_cali_en / cfg_gain_cali_en for correction (hal_adc_v154_regs_op.h:156-281; hal_adc_v154.c:73-178), never the raw cfg_offset/cfg_gain. grep finds no write to ->cfg_offset/->cfg_gain in the tree. PAC OffsetW/GainW = FieldWriter<16,u16> (ws63-pac/src/lib.rs:21913,21954).

surfaces.field: channel (per-channel enable bitmask) — implied GPIO pad provisioning
surfaces.issue: Enabling channel N sets lsadc_ctrl_0 bit N, but the analog input pad for that channel is not provisioned by the driver. The SDK, on channel-enable, also configures the corresponding GPIO (pull-disable + PIN_MODE_0 analog mux + input direction) on GPIO_07..GPIO_12 for channels 0..5. Without that pinmux/pull setup the channel reads a floating/driven digital pad, not the intended analog input: the scan 'works' but the data is meaningless. Unenforced precondition, not encoded in the type.
surfaces.evidence: hal_adc_v154_channel_set maps ADC_CHANNEL_0..5 -> GPIO_07..GPIO_12 and calls uapi_pin_set_pull(DISABLE)+uapi_pin_set_mode(PIN_MODE_0)+uapi_gpio_set_dir(INPUT) (hal_adc_v154.c:194-238). The Rust configure_scan only ORs the enable bit (lsadc.rs:144-155); no pinmux.

surfaces.issue: read_async enables IRQ72, starts the scan, and parks on ConvFuture until LSADC_SIGNAL fires (lsadc.rs:341-350). If the AFE/clock preconditions above are not met (held in reset, AFE not powered, pad not provisioned), the FIFO never fills, IRQ72 never fires, and the future parks forever; blocking callers spinning on data_ready() hang likewise. The vendor bounds every equivalent wait with COUNT_THRESHOLD=1000 iterations. No timeout/fallible signalling on the Rust side.
surfaces.evidence: SDK caps all FIFO/cali waits with COUNT_THRESHOLD=1000 (hal_adc_v154.c:33, used at :80-85,108-113,125-130,143-148,164-177,329-339). Rust ConvFuture has no bound (lsadc.rs:324-351); data_ready() reads rne with no timeout (lsadc.rs:175-177).

audits.notes:
Driver: /home/sanchuanhehe/Documents/hispark-rs/hisi-riscv-rs/crates/hisi-riscv-hal/src/lsadc.rs (v154 controller, base 0x4400_C000). All findings are READ-ONLY audit; not validated on silicon (the driver itself states this at lsadc.rs:21-26).

SAFE fields (checked, NOT flagged): start_count is u8 into an 8-bit start_cnt field (full width, no truncation, PAC StartCntW<8> ws63-pac:21287; default 0x18 fits). averaging is the Averaging enum (0..=3) into a 2-bit field — unrepresentable to overflow. The channel OR (1<<channel, channel<=5) into the 6-bit channel field cannot overflow. set_analog_enable bits / set_offset / set_gain are full-width u16 -> no truncation (their issues are effect/sequencing, classes B/C, not A). AdcChannel::from_index and parse_sample are correctly bounded (fuzz-tested). cfg_data_sel.data_sel and cic_filter_en are single bits.

CLOCK / RESET GATING (the load-bearing class-C gap): the reference PWM driver self-enables its clock tree in configure (CLK_SEL + CKEN_CTL0 + DIV_CTL3) so 'construct -> clocked'. LsAdc does NOT. The real LSADC gate is the CLDO soft-reset register cldo_crg_rst_soft_cfg_1 @ 0x4400_1138 (soft_rst_lsadc_n bit5, soft_rst_lsadc_bus_n bit7), a DIFFERENT peripheral the lsadc RegisterBlock does not reach. adc_port_clock_enable()/adc_port_init_clock() in the vendor porting are empty stubs — the soft-reset toggle is the only gate. enable() in the Rust driver releases da_lsadc_rstn (analog reset inside the lsadc block @ ctrl_11 bit16), which is necessary but NOT the bus clock/reset. This needs on-board confirmation: without the CLDO bus-reset deassert, none of the config writes are guaranteed to latch.

CHIP GATING: audited for ws63 only (v154 controller, hisi-riscv-rust ws63-pac). BS2X uses fbb_bs2x and may have a different ADC controller version / field layout — these citations do not transfer; re-audit separately for BS2X.

WIDTH-vs-SILICON caveat (PWM lesson): the class-A truncations (sample_cnt 5b, cast_cnt 7b, rxintsize 3b) are confirmed against BOTH the PAC FieldWriter widths AND the vendor regs_def bitfield widths, so they are real register-field caps, not datasheet-only. The recommended fix mirrors PWM: replace the raw u8 AdcConfig fields and the level/osr args with construct-time-validated newtypes (from_count -> Option) so an out-of-range value returns None instead of being silently masked. Whether the full PAC widths (e.g. cic_osr 8-bit, da_lsadc_en 16-bit) all LATCH on silicon is unconfirmed and needs a board, per the PWM high-half-doesn't-latch precedent.

surfaces.issue: data_bits is an unvalidated u8 written as ((data_bits-1) << 13) into a 5-bit field. data_bits >= 33 overflows the field: 33->raw 0x40000 truncates DFS to 0 (4-bit min frame) AND spills bit18 into CTRA.trsm, silently setting trsm=1 (TxOnly) so reads return zeros / full-duplex breaks; data_bits=255 gives DFS=30 (31-bit) AND trsm=3 (reserved/EEPROM-read). No None/Result is returned -- a structurally valid u8 silently produces the wrong frame size and a corrupted transfer mode on silicon.
surfaces.evidence: PAC ws63-pac/src/lib.rs:18931 Dfs32W FieldWriter<_,5> and :19021-19024 dfs32 = bits>>13 & 0x1f (5-bit field); vendor regs_def hal_spi_v151_regs_def.h:150 `uint32_t dfs32 : 5`. trsm is the adjacent field at bits 18:19 (lib.rs:19026), so any value >=32 in the dfs computation lands in trsm.

surfaces.field: data_bits: u8 -> SPI_CTRA.dfs32, valid silicon values
surfaces.issue: Even when it fits the 5-bit field, only data_bits=8 and data_bits=32 are actually runnable. The vendor explicitly rejects every other size (hal_spi_is_attr_valid: `frame_size != HAL_SPI_FRAME_SIZE_8 && frame_size != HAL_SPI_FRAME_SIZE_32` -> ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER). The driver accepts e.g. data_bits=9..31, 16, 24, 1..7: these program a legal DFS bit pattern but are unsupported by HiSilicon's HAL and, more concretely, the driver's own data path is byte-only (transfer/write push `byte as u32` one byte per FIFO slot and read `rx as u8`), so any frame >8 bits silently truncates/misaligns data on the wire (DesignWare right-justifies and zero-pads, so a 16/32-bit frame transmits only the low byte the driver wrote). data_bits=0 or 1 -> DFS field 0 = 4-bit minimum frame, not the requested width.
surfaces.evidence: vendor hal_spi_v151.c:85 `if (frame_size != HAL_SPI_FRAME_SIZE_8 && frame_size != HAL_SPI_FRAME_SIZE_32) return false`; hal_spi.h:196-205 HAL_SPI_FRAME_SIZE_8=0x07, _16=0x0F (Not supported now), _24=0x17 (Not supported now), _32=0x1F; driver spi.rs:169/173 byte-only FIFO (`write[i] as u32` / `rx as u8`).

surfaces.issue: sckdv computes (pclk/freq).clamp(2,0xFFFF) then masks bit0 even. Both ends are silently clamped instead of signalled: any frequency >= SPI_CLOCK_HZ/2 collapses to the same divisor 2 (max SCK = 80 MHz on WS63), and any frequency low enough that pclk/freq > 0xFFFE saturates to 0xFFFE (min SCK ~ 2.44 kHz) -- a caller asking for 1 kHz or 200 MHz gets a different real bus clock with no error. This matches the vendor clamp [2, 65534] so it is not a hardware-corrupting bug, but the requested vs actual frequency divergence is silent (defect D).
surfaces.evidence: PAC clk_div is 16-bit (lib.rs:19209 ClkDivW FieldWriter<_,16,u16>, :19211 bits 0:15); vendor caps match: hal_spi_v151_regs_def.h:172-173 HAL_SPI_MINUMUM_CLK_DIV=2 / HAL_SPI_MAXIMUM_CLK_DIV=65534, spi_porting.c:395-402 clamps clk_div to [2,65534]. Driver sckdv at spi.rs:52-56.

surfaces.field: SPI source clock + clock gate (CLDO_CRG) and SPI_CLOCK_HZ
surfaces.issue: The entire source-clock setup (configure_spi_source_clock: CRG divider + TCXO->PLL switch + CKEN_CTL1 bit25 gate-open) is behind `#[cfg(feature = "chip-ws63")]`. On BS2X the constructor programs SCKDV against soc::bs21::SPI_CLOCK_HZ but never establishes/verifies that input clock and never opens the SPI clock gate. SPI_CLOCK_HZ=32_000_000 is a TODO-marked 'assumed' value, so the SCKDV divisor is computed from an unconfirmed clock (wrong real SCK) and, if the gate is not opened elsewhere, the controller produces no output at all. Unenforced precondition: caller has no way to know the clock tree was not brought up.
surfaces.evidence: spi.rs:142-143 source-clock call gated to chip-ws63 only; spi.rs comment 135-141 explicitly defers BS2X source-clock to a TODO(bs2x); soc/bs21.rs:37-38 `SPI_CLOCK_HZ: u32 = 32_000_000` with `TODO(bs21): confirm (32 MHz assumed)`. Vendor ws63 gate sequence spi_porting.c:695-697 (CKEN_CTL1 bit25) has no BS2X analogue ported.

surfaces.field: CLDO_CRG_DIV_CTL3[9:5] source divider vs PLL-locked precondition
surfaces.issue: The function derives div = (480 / SSI_CLK_MHz).clamp(1,0x1F) and switches the SPI source to PLL, but assumes the 480 MHz FNPLL is already locked. There is no check that clock_init ran first; if the PLL is not up the switch to PLL (CLK_SEL bit6) yields no usable SSI_CLK and every transfer times out. The div clamp to [1,0x1F] silently caps if SPI_CLOCK_HZ were set below 480/31~15.5MHz (would alias to a wrong SSI_CLK), though with the shipped 160 MHz value div=3 is fine. Precondition (PLL locked) is documented in the doc-comment but not enforced.
surfaces.evidence: spi.rs:113-114 div = (480/ssi_mhz).clamp(1,0x1F); :109 doc 'Requires the PLL to already be locked'. CRG div field width 5 bits per vendor spi_porting.c:204 SPI_DIV_LEN=5, matching driver 0x1F mask; vendor spi_porting_clock_init:686-697 computes div with no clamp and assumes PLL up.

audits.notes: Config struct (spi.rs:18-29) has 3 fields: frequency:u32, mode:SpiMode, data_bits:u8. SpiMode is a closed 4-variant enum mapping only to valid CTRA scph/scpol bit combos (spi.rs:150-155) -- genuinely safe, no issue. The frequency clamp matches the vendor exactly (not hardware-corrupting, only silent divergence) so it is class D not A. The two real high-severity defects are both on data_bits: (A) values >=33 overflow the 5-bit dfs32 field AND spill into the adjacent trsm field corrupting transfer mode; (B) only 8 and 32 are vendor-supported and the driver's byte-only FIFO path means anything but 8 silently mistransmits even if the register accepts it. Recommended fix mirrors PWM/I2S newtype pattern: a construct-time-validated DataBits newtype (e.g. DataBits::new(u8)->Option accepting only 8, or 8|32 once a frame-width-aware data path exists) and a Frequency newtype that returns None instead of clamping, so an unrunnable Config is unrepresentable. UNCERTAIN / needs on-board confirmation: whether 32-bit frames are actually usable given the current byte-only transfer/write/transfer_in_place loops -- on silicon a DFS=32 frame would shift 32 bits but the driver only loads 1 byte per FIFO entry, so 32-bit support needs both a new data path and a HIL check; vendor lists 16/24 as 'Not supported now' so do not widen the type to them. The WS63 SCKDV bit0-is-read-only-0 (even) constraint is handled (spi.rs:55) and matches the silicon-validated SPI_WSR note in the PAC. BS2X SPI has never been brought up on silicon (TODO markers throughout); treat all BS2X SPI clocking as unconfirmed.

surfaces.method: new / configure / enable / set_load (every method that touches the block)
surfaces.issue: The v100 RTC at 0x5702_4000 is an always-on (AON) peripheral with NO software clock-gate; it is driven directly by the external 32.768 kHz source. The HAL never enables a clock (RTC is absent from clock.rs Peripheral enum; rtc_porting.c has no CKEN sequence), so 'construct -> clocked' silently relies on the board having the 32k crystal populated. On this EVB the 32.768 kHz crystal is NOT populated: the counter never advances (current_value() stuck), interrupts never fire, and per the bring-up note any access to the RTC block stalls the bus / hangs the core. new() and configure() take the peripheral and poke registers with no guard or precondition, so a structurally-valid call is silently unrunnable (and worse, hangs) on an unprovisioned board.
surfaces.evidence: CONFIG_RTC_CLOCK_VALUE=32768 (fbb_ws63 drivers/chips/ws63/porting/rtc_unified/CMakeLists.txt:25); no RTC variant in hisi-riscv-hal/src/clock.rs Peripheral enum (lines 27-45) and no cken_info bit; rtc_porting.c (whole file) has no clock-enable, only IRQ wiring; PAC base 0x5702_4000 (ws63-pac/src/lib.rs:24305). Bring-up memory: 'this EVB lacks 32.768kHz crystal; touching RTC stalls the bus.' Needs on-board confirmation of crystal population before any RTC use.

surfaces.issue: RtcMode::Periodic sets control bit1=1. The vendor v100 mode field only defines RTC_MODE_FREE_RUN=0 and RTC_MODE_USER_DEF=1 ('user define mode'), and the vendor SDK NEVER programs mode=1 for normal operation: hal_rtc_v100_config_load unconditionally forces mode=RTC_MODE_FREE_RUN (0) on every load. So Periodic selects an undocumented/unexercised hardware mode whose silicon behavior is not attested by any vendor path. The driver presents it as a first-class, equally-valid option (the whole RtcMode enum + control_bits encoder + test suite treat Periodic as supported), but it produces no SDK-validated counting/interrupt behavior. Structurally valid, no attested working output.
surfaces.evidence: hal_rtc_v100.h:33-42 (enum control_reg_mode { RTC_MODE_FREE_RUN=0, RTC_MODE_USER_DEF=1 }); hal_rtc_v100.c:50-54 hal_rtc_v100_config_load always calls hal_rtc_control_reg_set_mode(index, RTC_MODE_FREE_RUN). PAC rtc_control.mode is a single bit (ws63-pac/src/lib.rs:24436-24439). Needs on-board confirmation of what mode=1 actually does.

surfaces.issue: load_value of 0 compiles and is written straight to RTC_LOAD_COUNT, then the counter is enabled. A zero reload value gives a degenerate / immediate-expiry counter (no usable interval); the vendor driver explicitly rejects this. The HAL silently accepts it instead of signalling — a caller computing load from a duration that rounds to 0 gets a non-functioning timer with no error.
surfaces.evidence: Vendor uapi_rtc_start rejects rtc_ms==0 with ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAM (rtc.c:461); uapi_rtc_start_hw_rtc likewise rejects rtc_ms==0 (rtc.c:575). The HAL configure()/set_load() (rtc.rs:74-84, 103-107) have no zero check.

surfaces.field: load_value written while counter enabled (no stop/reload ordering)
surfaces.issue: set_load() writes RTC_LOAD_COUNT directly without first disabling the counter. Every vendor reload path stops the counter (hal_rtc_stop) before writing load_count and re-starts (hal_rtc_start) afterward; writing the reload value on a live free-running counter is the exact sequence the vendor avoids and can latch the new period only on the next wrap (or produce a glitched first interval). The unenforced precondition (disable before set_load) is invisible at the API; configure() is safe only because it writes load before the control word that enables, but set_load() offers no such guarantee.
surfaces.evidence: Vendor always brackets reload with stop/start: rtc.c:486-488 (hal_rtc_stop; rtc_load_cycle_with_mask; hal_rtc_start) and rtc.c:580-586 / 651-653. The HAL set_load() (rtc.rs:103-107) writes load_count with no preceding disable.

audits.notes: Scope: chip-ws63 selects rtc.rs (v100 IP); rtc_v150.rs is the BS2X/bs21 build (different 64-bit IP, gated by chip-bs21) and is out of scope for the ws63 audit. Config/construction surfaces in rtc.rs are new(), configure(mode, load_value), set_load(load_value), plus the boolean toggles enable/disable/enable_interrupt/disable_interrupt (those write only single, full-width-safe control bits — no overflow possible, no issue). No defect-class-A (register-field overflow) exists here: RTC_LOAD_COUNT and RTC_CURRENT_VALUE are genuine full-width 32-bit registers (PAC Ux=u32, single reg; vendor volatile uint32_t at regs_def.h:58-59), and the public API types them as u32, so the documented '48-bit counter' is a doc/const fiction (RTC_COUNTER_WIDTH=48 in soc/ws63.rs:150 and the rtc.rs module doc) but the API width is correct — flag the doc, not the type. The control register is three single-bit fields, all safely encoded. The dominant, deploy-blocking issue is the class-C crystal precondition: on a board without the 32.768 kHz crystal (this EVB) the RTC has no clock and, per bring-up notes, accessing it stalls the bus — recommend a board-feature/precondition gate (e.g. a typestate or a fallible constructor that the BSP can only hand out when the 32k source is provisioned) rather than an unconditional new(). All hardware-behavior claims (Periodic/mode=1 semantics, live-reload glitch, bus-stall on missing crystal) are inferred from the vendor SDK + bring-up memory and need on-silicon confirmation; the audit did not touch hardware.

surfaces.issue: Any timeout_ms whose cycle count exceeds the 24-bit field is SILENTLY clamped to WDT_MAX_LOAD (line 119: `.min(WDT_MAX_LOAD as u64)`) instead of being signalled. On WS63 (24 MHz) the cap is ~179 s; on BS2X/BS21 (32 MHz) it is only ~134 s. A caller requesting e.g. configure(300_000, ...) gets a ~179 s / ~134 s watchdog with NO error — the system resets far sooner than the requested window, which is exactly the dangerous direction for a safety watchdog. The vendor rejects this: hal_watchdog_v151_set_attr returns ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAM when timeout_s > TOP_MAX_CLKS (and on multiply overflow).
surfaces.evidence: WDT_LOAD field is 24-bit at bits[31:8] (ws63-pac lib.rs:23857 `WdtLoadW<..,24,u32>`; bs2x-pac lib.rs:4817 identical). Effective cycles = field<<8, so max timeout = (0xFFFFFF<<8)/clk = ~178.96 s @24 MHz, ~134.2 s @32 MHz. Vendor floor/ceiling + overflow rejection: hal_watchdog_v151.c:64-71 (TOP_MIN_CLKS 0xFF / TOP_MAX_CLKS 0xFFFFFFFF, overflow check, return ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAM).

surfaces.issue: configure(0, ...) is accepted and programs WDT_LOAD = 0 (cycles=0, field=0), then sets wdt_en=1. A zero reload makes the down-counter expire immediately / on the first tick, producing an instant-reset (or non-functional) watchdog rather than a usable timeout. The driver's own unit test `zero_timeout_yields_zero_load` even codifies load=0 as acceptable. The vendor explicitly forbids this: set_attr's `(timeout == 0)` branch returns ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAM, and TOP_MIN_CLKS=0xFF enforces a minimum cycle count. (Note: because the smallest non-zero timeout_ms=1 already yields ~24000/32000 cycles, the load=0 hazard is reachable only at timeout_ms=0; sub-TOP_MIN loads are otherwise unreachable via the ms API.)
surfaces.evidence: hal_watchdog_v151.c:65 `if (((timeout == 0) || ...) == 0) return ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAM;` and :69 `if (timeout_s < TOP_MIN_CLKS ...) return ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAM;` (TOP_MIN_CLKS=0xFF, hal_watchdog_v151.c:21). Driver has no min/zero guard: wdt.rs:113-119, 125.

surfaces.issue: Neither new() nor configure() enables the WDT clock gate or releases its soft-reset; the driver assumes the AON clock to the WDT is already running. This is an unenforced precondition: 'construct -> clocked' is NOT guaranteed by the type, unlike the PWM reference pattern which self-enables its clock tree in configure. In practice the WDT sits in the always-on (AON) domain and the gate is reset-default-on (the vendor watchdog_turnon_clk() porting hook is a no-op and clock_init never touches wdt_gate), so this is latent rather than always-broken — but a caller who has gated the AON WDT clock (low-power experiments) gets a silently dead watchdog with no diagnostic.
surfaces.evidence: Clock-gate field exists: ws63-pac lib.rs:25257-25274 (wdt_gate AON_CRG_CKEN_CTL[4]) and :25339-25356 (wdt_soft_rst AON_SOFT_RST_CTL[1]). Vendor relies on boot defaults: watchdog_porting.c:35-43 watchdog_turnon_clk/turnoff_clk are empty no-ops. HAL clock.rs Peripheral enum has no Wdt/cken_info entry; wdt.rs new()/configure() never write any CRG register.

surfaces.field: timeout_ms -> cycles, using WDT_CLOCK_HZ = soc::chip::TCXO_HZ (32 MHz on BS2X/BS21)
surfaces.issue: The ms->cycles conversion hardcodes WDT_CLOCK_HZ = TCXO_HZ for whatever chip is selected (24 MHz WS63, 32 MHz BS21). On WS63 this is confirmed against the vendor (CONFIG_WDT_CLOCK = 24000000). For BS2X/BS21 the WDT counting clock is ASSUMED to equal the 32 MHz TCXO but is NOT verified against a BS2X vendor source (no fbb_bs2x watchdog porting present in this tree). If the BS2X WDT actually counts at a different rate (e.g. a divided TCXO or a 24 MHz reference like WS63), every timeout_ms is silently mis-scaled — the programmed timeout would be off by the clock ratio with no error. This is the same 'type/constant encodes the datasheet, not measured silicon' risk as the PWM pwm_freq-high-half lesson; needs on-board confirmation for BS2X.
